1.1:
O the blessedness of the one
who does not walk by the counsel of the wicked,
who does not linger in the path of sinners,
who does not take a seat among the scornful.
1.2:
His heart finds its delight in the LORD's teaching,
and on that teaching he ponders
by day and through the night.
1.3:
He is like a tree
rooted by channels of water—
bearing fruit in every season,
its leaves never failing—
and whatever he undertakes thrives.
1.4:
The wicked are nothing like this:
they are like chaff the wind scatters.
1.5:
So the wicked will not hold their ground when judgment comes,
nor will sinners find a place in the company of the righteous.
1.6:
For the LORD knows and guards the path of the righteous,
but the road the wicked travel leads to ruin.

2.1:
Why this uproar among the nations?
Why do the peoples hatch their empty schemes?
2.3:
"We will shatter the fetters they have laid on us
and hurl their restraints away!"
2.4:
The one enthroned in heaven laughs;
the Lord God mocks their scheming.
2.5:
Then in his fury he rebukes them,
startling them with the blaze of his wrath:
2.6:
"Yet I have enthroned my King
on Zion, my holy mountain."
2.8:
Ask it of me, and the nations become your inheritance;
the farthest reaches of the earth are yours.
2.9:
You will shatter them with an iron scepter
and smash them like a potter's jar.
2.10:
Therefore, kings, act wisely;
heed the warning, all you who govern the earth.
2.11:
Serve the LORD in holy awe
and rejoice before him with trembling.
2.12:
Do homage to the Son before his anger kindles—
for that wrath can flare in a moment.
How blessed are all who shelter in him.

3.1:
A Psalm of David — written when he fled from Absalom his son.

LORD, how countless my enemies have become!
How many rise up against me!
3.2:
On every side the word goes round:
'God will not rescue this one.' Selah
3.3:
But you, LORD, surround me like a shield—
you are my honor, the one who holds my head high.
3.4:
I called out to the LORD,
and he answered from his holy mountain. Selah
3.5:
I lay down and slept,
and woke again—
because the LORD held me safe.
3.6:
Though ten thousand enemies encamp around me,
I will not be afraid.
3.7:
Rise up, LORD! Rescue me, my God!
For you strike my enemies across the face
and snap the teeth of the wicked.
3.8:
Deliverance belongs to the LORD alone—
may your blessing rest upon your people. Selah

4.1:
To the worship leader: for stringed instruments. A Psalm of David.

Answer me when I call, O God who vindicates me!
You opened a way out when I was hemmed in.
Be merciful to me now and hear my prayer.
4.2:
You people—how long will you make my honor a target for contempt?
How long will you chase after empty things and run after what is false? Selah
4.3:
Know this: the LORD has claimed the faithful as his own;
he hears me when I call.
4.4:
Let holy fear hold you—and do not sin.
Ponder your ways in the quiet of the night,
and be still. Selah
4.5:
Bring honest offerings to God
and anchor your hope in the LORD.
4.6:
People ask, 'Where will we find any good?'
Let your face shine on us, LORD—that is the only answer.
4.7:
The gladness you have placed in my heart
exceeds all they feel when the harvest overflows.
4.8:
I lie down and fall asleep in peace,
for you alone, LORD, let me rest secure.

5.2:
Hear my cry for help, my King and my God—
it is to you alone I bring my prayer.
5.4:
You are a God who takes no pleasure in evil;
wickedness finds no welcome in your presence.
5.5:
Arrogance cannot survive your gaze;
you despise all who live by lawlessness.
5.7:
But I—through the flood of your steadfast love—
I will enter your house.
I bow toward your holy temple in the fear that becomes me.
5.10:
Declare them guilty, O God;
let their own plots be their downfall.
Cast them out for all their lawless deeds—
they have risen in open revolt against you.
5.11:
But let everyone who shelters in you burst into song;
let them shout their joy without end.
You spread your covering over them—
and all who love your name exult in your presence.
5.12:
For you, LORD, bless the one who is right with you;
you wrap him in your favor like a shield.

6.2:
Have mercy on me, LORD—I am utterly spent;
heal me, LORD, for my very bones ache with dread.
6.3:
My whole self is shaking to its depths.
And you, LORD—how long will you wait?
6.4:
Come back to me, LORD—rescue my life!
Save me, because of your steadfast love.
6.5:
In death no one calls your name to mind;
in the grave, who can still sing your praise?
6.7:
My eyes are blurred with weeping,
haggard from watching my enemies close in.
6.8:
Away from me, all you who practice evil!
The LORD has heard me weeping.
6.9:
The LORD has heard my cry for help;
the LORD takes up my prayer as his own.

7.1:
A Shiggaion — a passionate lament — that David sang to the LORD, prompted by the accusations of Cush the Benjaminite.

O LORD, you are my God. I run to you for shelter. Save me. Rescue me from everyone who is hunting me down.
7.2:
If you don't act, my life is finished —
they will rip it open like a lion shreds its prey,
and no one will be there to pull me free.
7.3: LORD my God, here is my oath: if I have done what they claim, if my hands carry any such guilt —
7.4: if I have betrayed a friend who trusted me, if I have plundered someone who had done me no harm —
7.5:
then let my enemies catch me.
Let them grind my life into the dirt.
Let everything I stand for be buried in the dust. Selah
7.6:
Rise up, LORD! Let your anger do what it must.
Stand against my enemies' rage.
You are the one who has decreed justice — now see it through!
7.7:
Let the nations assemble in your court.
Then take your throne above them all.
7.8:
You are the judge of every nation — judge me too, LORD,
by the measure of my righteousness, by the integrity you know is in me.
7.9:
Bring the wicked's career of evil to its end.
But let the righteous stand firm.
You are the God who searches inward — every heart, every motive — and you are just.
7.10:
God himself — the Most High — is the shield that covers me,
the one who rescues every honest soul.
7.11:
God is a judge — a just one.
Every single day, wickedness provokes his righteous anger.
7.12:
He has sharpened his sword.
He has bent the bow and set the string.
He waits — but if the wicked will not turn, he is ready.
7.13:
His arsenal is stocked — weapons made for the kill.
His arrows are aflame, aimed at those who drive others down.
7.14:
Watch how the wicked work:
They conceive their scheme, carry it to term,
and what they finally deliver is one more lie.
7.15:
He dug a trap.
He made it deep.
Then he fell into it himself.
7.16:
All his cruelty loops back on him.
The violence he launched lands on his own head.
7.17:
For this — for all of it — I will praise him.
I will sing to the LORD who is just.
I will lift my voice to the name of the LORD Most High.

8.1:
A Psalm of David, for the choirmaster, to the melody of 'the Gittith.'

LORD — our Lord —
how magnificent your name is across all the earth!
Your splendor towers above the heavens themselves.
8.2:
Even the voices of children — of babies still at the breast —
you have shaped into a fortress of praise,
to stop the mouths of your enemies, to silence every rebel cry.
8.3:
When I look up at the night sky — your sky —
at the moon, at those uncountable stars,
the intricate handiwork of your fingers —
8.4:
— what is a human being, that you would spend a thought on us?
Who are we — this fleeting dust — that you would pay us any personal attention?
8.5:
Yet you made us barely a step below the divine.
You set glory like a crown on our heads.
You invested us with honor.
8.6:
You have put him in charge of everything your hands have made.
Every created thing — you have laid it at his feet:
8.7:
herds and flocks, every domesticated creature,
and all the wildlife of the open country,
8.8:
birds overhead, fish threading through the sea's dark paths —
everything that moves in the deep channels of the ocean.
8.9:
LORD — our Lord —
how magnificent your name is across all the earth!

9.1:
A Psalm of David, for the choirmaster, to be sung to 'Muth-labben.'

With everything in me — my whole heart — I will praise you, LORD.
I will tell the full account of every wonder you have done.
9.2:
In you I find a joy that rises to exultation.
To you, Most High, I will sing.
9.3:
When my enemies turn and run,
they trip, they crumble — right there before your face.
9.4:
You stepped in for me — you took up my case as your own.
From your throne you rendered justice: the right verdict, the only verdict.
9.5:
You confronted the nations and they broke.
You destroyed the wicked — and more: you erased their name,
wiped it clean so that nothing of them remains, now or ever.
9.6:
The enemy is finished — buried in permanent ruin.
You tore their cities out by the roots.
Nothing remains. Not even a memory.
9.7:
But the LORD — he endures.
He sits on his throne forever.
He has fixed that throne as the seat of justice.
9.8:
He will judge the whole world — and he will do it right.
Every people, every nation: he will deal with them in perfect fairness.
9.9:
For those being crushed — the ground-down, the powerless —
the LORD is a high fortress.
When trouble closes in, he is the place to run.
9.10:
Knowing you — truly knowing who you are — is the surest ground for trust.
You have never once abandoned anyone who came looking for you, LORD. Not once.
9.11:
Sing to the LORD, the God who makes Zion his home!
Broadcast what he has done — let every nation hear it!
9.12:
He takes note of every act of bloodshed.
He holds in memory every cry that ever came from the afflicted.
Not one of those cries has been forgotten.
9.13:
Have mercy on me, LORD. Look at what hatred does to a person.
You are the one who can haul me back from death's own gate —
9.14:
— so that I can stand in the gates of Zion and tell the full story:
how you saved me. Every detail. And I will be glad.
9.15:
The nations dug a pit.
They fell into it.
They laid a trap.
Their own feet walked into it.
9.16:
The LORD has made himself unmistakable:
he does justice, and the wicked are caught — caught in the very thing they built to trap others.
[Higgaion — a meditation. Pause and reflect.] Selah
9.17:
The wicked are headed for Sheol — the place of the forgotten dead.
Every nation that has turned its back on God will follow them there.
9.18:
But the poor will not be erased from memory.
The hope of the afflicted will not be deferred forever.
It will not perish.
9.19:
Rise up, LORD — do not let mere human arrogance win the day.
Bring the nations before your court.
9.20:
Put the fear of you into them, LORD.
Let the nations discover what they truly are — mortal, finite, just men. Selah

10.1:
LORD, why are you so far away?
Why do you hide — especially now, when trouble has swallowed everything?
10.2:
With pride as his engine, the wicked hunts the helpless.
Let him be snared in the very traps he designed.
10.3:
The wicked is proud of what he wants — he boasts about his appetites.
The greedy man curses the LORD and dismisses him without a thought.
10.4:
The wicked, in his pride, never once looks up toward God.
His entire frame of thought has no room for God — none.
10.5:
Everything he does seems to work — at least for now.
Your judgments are far above him; he cannot see them, never looks for them.
As for his enemies, he blows at them like smoke.
10.6:
He tells himself: 'I am immovable. Nothing will shake me.
No setback will ever reach me — not now, not ever.'
10.7:
His every word is a weapon — curses, lies, manipulation.
Hidden beneath his tongue: harm he is already planning, evil he is about to do.
10.8:
He lies in wait near the villages — in the shadows, watching.
He kills the innocent.
His eyes are fixed on the vulnerable, tracking them.
10.9:
He crouches in the underbrush like a lion before it springs.
He lunges — and the poor person is caught,
dragged into the net before they even knew it was there.
10.10: He coils. He pounces. And the helpless collapse under him.
10.11:
He assures himself: 'God has forgotten all about this.
He has looked away. He will never see what I am doing.'
10.12:
Rise up, LORD! Raise your hand, O God!
Do not forget the people who have no one else.
10.13: How can the wicked go on dismissing you, LORD — telling himself you will not act, will not demand an answer?
10.14:
But you do see — you see it all.
Trouble, grief, every grievance — you take it all in and hold it.
The helpless have nowhere else to go but you,
and you have always been the one who defends the fatherless.
10.15:
Shatter the power of the wicked.
Pursue every last thread of their evil until nothing remains — until there is nothing left to find.
10.16:
The LORD reigns. He has always reigned. He will always reign.
And those nations that have lived by violence — they will be swept from his land.
10.17:
LORD, you have heard what the humble long for.
You strengthen their resolve. You lean in to listen.
10.18:
You act for the orphan, for the crushed.
You make sure that no mere mortal can go on terrorizing others without a reckoning.

11.1:
A Psalm of David, for the choirmaster.

The LORD is where I shelter. So why do you keep urging me to flee — 'Get out, like a bird escaping to the hills!'
11.2:
Yes — the wicked have strung their bows.
They have arrows on the string, aimed and ready.
They intend to shoot from the shadows at those whose hearts are right.
11.3: And if everything foundational collapses — what ground is left for the righteous to stand on?
11.4:
But the LORD is in his holy temple.
His throne is fixed in heaven.
His eyes are open — they see everything.
His searching gaze examines every human being.
11.5:
He tests the righteous — that testing is his way of knowing them.
But the wicked, the violence-lovers — his whole being recoils from them.
11.6:
On the wicked he rains down fire and burning sulfur.
A searing wind — that is what fills their cup to the brim.
11.7:
The LORD is just — that is who he is.
He loves justice — it is what he does.
And those who are truly upright? They will see him face to face.

12.1:
A Psalm of David, for the choirmaster, to be played on the lower eight-stringed instrument.

Lord, we need you — urgently. The person of integrity has disappeared.
The loyal have become an endangered species. Where have they all gone?
12.2:
Lies are the common currency now.
Everyone flatters their neighbor while thinking something entirely different —
a smooth face, a divided heart.
12.3:
Let the LORD silence those smooth tongues.
Let him cut off every mouth that flatters and brags.
12.4:
They are saying: 'Words are our weapons. We will win with our mouths.
Who has any authority over us?'
12.5:
'Enough,' says the LORD. 'The poor are being robbed. The needy are crying out.
Now I am rising. I will set them in the safety they have longed for.'
12.6:
What the LORD says is nothing like human speech.
His words are pure: silver smelted down, refined until only the finest remains —
purified seven times over. Flawless.
12.7:
You will keep your people, LORD.
You will hold us against everything this corrupt generation tries to do.
12.8:
The wicked strut freely on every side,
while what is depraved is celebrated — that is the world we are living in.

13.1:
To the choirmaster. Of David.
How long, LORD? Forever?
How long will you turn your face away from me?
13.2:
How long must I carry this grief inside me,
this daily weight of sorrow?
How long will the enemy stand over me in triumph?
13.3:
Look at me, LORD—answer me.
Brighten my eyes before they close in the sleep of death;
13.4:
lest the enemy crow, 'I have broken him,'—
lest my foes celebrate the moment I stumble and fall.
13.5:
But I have staked everything on your steadfast love.
My heart will yet burst into joy at your rescue.
13.6:
I will sing to the LORD.
He has dealt generously with me—I know it even now.

14.1:
To the choirmaster. Of David.
The morally bankrupt say to themselves, 'God doesn't count.'
And so they become corrupt, their deeds repugnant—
not one of them does what is good.
14.2:
From heaven the LORD surveys the whole human family—
scanning for anyone who grasps reality, who actually seeks him.
14.3:
Every one of them has turned off the path.
Every one has curdled.
Not one does good—not a single one.
14.4:
Have they no understanding at all—these agents of ruin
who consume my people like bread at a meal,
who never once think to call on God?
14.5:
Then terror seized them—sudden, overwhelming—
because God stands with those who are faithful to him.
14.6:
You would humiliate the powerless and undermine them—
but the LORD himself is their shelter.
14.7:
When will the rescue come—from Zion, where God dwells?
When the LORD reverses his people's captivity,
Jacob will burst out laughing and Israel will dance.

15.1:
Of David.
LORD, who belongs in your house?
Who has the right to stand on your holy mountain?
15.2:
The one whose life is whole and consistent—
who does what is just and who means what they say.
15.3:
Someone who keeps their tongue clean of slander,
who does nothing to hurt those around them,
and refuses to pick up a shaming story about a friend.
15.4:
One who sees through what is worthless and honors those who revere God—
who takes an oath and keeps it even when it costs dearly,
never looking for the exit.
15.5:
Who never charges interest on a loan to someone in need,
and cannot be bought to condemn the innocent.
This is the person who stands—unshakeable, forever.

16.1:
A Miktam—of David.
Keep me safe, God. You are where I have hidden myself.
16.2:
I have said to the LORD: You are my Master.
Every good thing I have exists only in you.
16.3:
The faithful ones scattered through the earth—
they are the magnificent, and I love them all.
16.4:
Those who rush headlong after rival gods will multiply their own grief.
I will not join their rituals—
not a syllable of those names will cross my lips.
16.5:
The LORD is my share—the portion I have been given.
He holds the cup I will drink from.
My future is in his hands.
16.6:
The surveyor's lines have measured out a beautiful piece of land for me.
What a heritage I have been given.
16.7:
I praise the LORD who advises me—
even through the night my deepest self speaks with wisdom.
16.8:
I have deliberately placed the LORD always in front of me.
With him at my right side, nothing can knock me down.
16.9:
So my heart is full of gladness,
my whole being sings with joy—
and even this body rests in safety.
16.10:
Because you will not abandon me to the realm of the dead—
you will not let your beloved one rot in the grave.
16.11:
You open the path of life before me.
In your presence—joy beyond all other joy.
At your right hand, pleasures that will never end.

17.1:
A Prayer—of David.
Hear me, LORD—this is a righteous plea.
Listen to my cry. I am not lying to you.
17.2:
Let my acquittal come directly from you—
your eyes can see the truth of it.
17.3:
You have put my heart to the test—
a night examination—and found nothing to charge me with.
I have made a firm decision: my mouth will not betray me.
17.4:
When it comes to human behavior,
your word has been my compass—steering me away from the road of the violent.
17.5:
I have stayed on your tracks.
My feet have not slid off.
17.6:
I am calling you—and you will answer. I know it.
Turn your ear toward me, God. Listen.
17.7:
Show what your steadfast love can do—
you who shelter by your right hand those who flee to you
from those who rise against them.
17.8:
Guard me like the pupil of your eye—
that small, perfectly protected point at the center.
Shelter me beneath your wings,
17.9:
from violent people who press in on me,
from enemies who would kill me if they could—circling.
17.10:
They have sealed themselves against compassion.
Their mouths overflow with boasting.
17.11:
They have closed in all around me now.
Their eyes are fixed on one goal: bringing me down to the earth.
17.12:
Like a lion that can think of nothing but the kill—
a young lion invisible in the shadows, waiting.
17.13:
Arise, LORD! Face him. Strike him down.
Draw your sword and pull me out from the grip of the wicked—
17.14:
with your hand, save me from people whose whole world is this world alone—
who get everything they want right here, right now.
You fill them full anyway; their children inherit plenty,
and they pass the surplus down yet another generation.
But that is all they will ever get.
17.15:
But my satisfaction is different:
I will see your face—not just sense your presence, but see it clearly.
When I awake, I will be full—satisfied by your very image.

18.1:
To the choirmaster. Of David, servant of the LORD—written for the day the LORD rescued him from the grip of all his enemies and from Saul.
I love you, LORD. You are my strength.
18.2:
The LORD is my rock, my fortress, my rescuer—
my God is the cliff I hide in.
He is my shield, the source of my victory, my high tower.
18.3:
I called on the LORD—who deserves every praise—
and he saved me from those who were coming for me.
18.4:
The ropes of death wound around me;
chaos came at me like a flood.
18.5:
Sheol wound its own cords around me;
death's traps were laid directly in my path.
18.6:
Pressed into a corner, I called on the LORD.
I cried out to my God.
From his temple he heard my voice—
my desperate cry went straight into his ears.
18.7:
The earth heaved and shook.
The deep roots of the mountains quaked.
He was furious—and the ground knew it.
18.8:
Smoke billowed from his nostrils.
Fire poured from his mouth and consumed everything.
Coals blazed ahead of him.
18.9:
He bent the sky downward and descended—
storm cloud his footstool.
18.10:
He rode a cherub at full speed,
flying on the wind's own wings.
18.11:
He wrapped himself in darkness—
storm clouds, black and heavy with water, his pavilion.
18.12:
His own brilliance split the clouds apart—
hailstones and burning coals blazing out ahead of him.
18.13:
God thundered across the sky.
The Most High raised his voice—
and the air itself became hail and fire.
18.14:
He launched his arrows and broke their formation.
Lightning—and they collapsed into panic.
18.15:
The ocean floor appeared.
The bedrock of the world was uncovered—
all from one word of rebuke, one blast of breath from your nostrils.
18.16:
He reached down from above and grabbed me.
He pulled me out of the deep flood.
18.17:
He pulled me free of my enemy—powerful, dangerous—
from those who hated me, who were far too strong for me to face alone.
18.18:
They came for me at my worst moment.
But the LORD held me up.
18.19:
He brought me out into open ground—
room to breathe, room to stand.
He did it because he wanted to.
18.20:
The LORD treated me the way I had been living—
my hands were clean, and he knew it.
18.21:
I walked his road.
I did not abandon my God in the crisis.
18.22:
His decrees were always in front of me.
I never put them behind me.
18.23:
Before him I was blameless—
and I kept myself away from my own tendency to sin.
18.24:
And so the LORD returned to me what I had given to him.
His eyes saw my hands were clean.
18.25:
To the loyal, you are loyal.
To the blameless, you are blameless.
18.26:
To the pure, you are pure.
But with the twisted, you become the one who outsmarts them—
you meet each person where they are.
18.27:
You lift up the poor and lowly.
The ones who strut—you bring them down.
18.28:
You are the one who keeps my lamp burning.
You push back my darkness, LORD.
18.29:
With you behind me, I can storm a fortified position.
With my God, I can clear any wall.
18.30:
This God is flawless in everything he does.
His word has been refined to perfection—nothing false in it.
He is a shield to everyone who runs to him.
18.31:
Who else is God? No one but the LORD.
Who else is a Rock? No one but our God.
18.32:
He is the one who girds me with strength
and sets my path straight.
18.33:
He gives me the agility of a mountain deer—
surefootedness on ground that would make others fall.
18.34:
He has trained my hands for war—
my arms can now bend what no one bends unaided.
18.35:
You handed me the shield of your rescue.
Your right arm held me up.
And here is the surprise: your gentleness is what made me great.
18.36:
You widened the ground beneath my feet—
there was room to plant myself, and I did not fall.
18.37:
I ran my enemies down.
I did not stop until every one of them was done.
18.38:
I hit them so hard they could not get up.
They went down at my feet.
18.39:
You were the one who gave me strength for the fight.
You brought my enemies down beneath me.
18.40:
You made them run.
I finished off those who hated me.
18.41:
They screamed for help—no one came.
They even called on the LORD, but there was no answer for them.
18.42:
I ground them to dust the wind could scatter.
I threw them away like street filth.
18.43:
You pulled me out of quarrels within my own people
and set me as head over nations I had never known—
strangers who became my subjects.
18.44:
Word of me reached them—and they submitted.
Strangers came before me in deference.
18.45:
The fight went out of them.
They came stumbling out of their strongholds, shaking.
18.46:
The LORD is alive!
Honor to my Rock!
Let the God who saved me be lifted high—
18.47:
the God who settles accounts on my behalf
and brings nations to their knees before me.
18.48:
He pulled me out from under my enemies.
You raised me above those who came to take me down.
You saved me from the violent ones.
18.49:
This is why I will praise you among all peoples, LORD—
I will sing your name out loud, everywhere.
18.50:
He gives sweeping victories to his king.
He shows steadfast love to his anointed—
to David and his line, forever and beyond.
The covenant holds.

19.2:
Day passes knowledge to day;
night hands its wisdom on to night.
19.3:
No words are spoken, no language used,
no voice is heard aloud—
19.4:
and yet their signal reaches every corner of the earth,
their wordless message travels to the world's edge.
There in the heavens God has pitched a tent for the sun,
19.5:
who rises like a bridegroom stepping from his wedding chamber,
exulting like a champion who cannot wait to run.
19.7:
The LORD's instruction is flawless—it revives the inner life;
the LORD's testimony is reliable—it grants wisdom to the untaught.
19.8:
The LORD's precepts are just—they fill the heart with joy;
the LORD's commandment is clear—it opens the eyes to see.
19.9:
The reverence the LORD calls forth is undefiled—it endures forever;
the LORD's verdicts are utterly true, entirely just.
19.10:
More precious than gold they are—than heaps of purest gold;
sweeter than honey, than honey fresh from the comb.
19.11:
By them your servant is warned against what harms;
in obeying them lies great and lasting reward.
19.12:
Who can even see all his own failures?
Declare me free of the sins I cannot detect.
19.13:
Hold your servant back from deliberate defiance—
do not let those sins become my masters.
Then I will be whole and unblemished,
free from the weight of flagrant rebellion.
19.14:
May every word from my mouth
and every movement of my heart
find acceptance before you, LORD—
my rock, my redeemer.

20.2:
May help reach you from the sanctuary;
may Zion be the source of your strength.
20.3:
May he keep every offering you have brought before him,
accepting your burnt gifts with favor. Selah
20.4:
May he give you the very thing your heart longs for
and bring every plan you have laid to completion.
20.7:
Let others put their confidence in chariots,
let others count on cavalry—
we invoke the name of the LORD our God.
20.8:
They stumble and go down;
we rise and hold our ground.
20.9:
LORD, give the king victory!
Answer us the moment we call.

21.2:
You gave him the longing of his heart;
you held back nothing that his lips asked for. Selah
21.3:
You came out to meet him laden with good gifts;
you set upon his head a crown of purest gold.
21.4:
He asked for life—you gave it:
length of days without end, age upon age.
21.5:
His glory grows great through the victory you have granted;
you have wrapped him in splendor and royal dignity.
21.6:
You have made him a source of blessing without end;
you make him radiant with joy in your presence.
21.7:
The king's trust is fixed in the LORD;
through the unfailing love of the Most High he stands unmoved.
21.8:
Your hand will track down every enemy;
your right arm will close on all who hate you.
21.10:
You will wipe their offspring from the face of the earth,
their descendants from the human race.
21.11:
They hatched evil schemes against you
and laid malicious plans—
but every plot dissolves before it forms.
21.12:
You send them fleeing in retreat;
your arrows are already aimed at their faces.
21.13:
Rise in your strength, O LORD!
We will sing—we will make music to your might.

22.1:
To the worship leader: to the tune 'The Doe of the Dawn.' A Psalm of David.

My God, my God — why have you abandoned me?
Why are you so far, unable to save me,
so far from the words of my groaning?
22.2:
By day I cry out to you, my God—
but no answer comes.
By night I cry—
and there is no rest for me.
22.3:
And yet — you are holy,
enthroned upon the songs of praise Israel raises to you.
22.4:
Our ancestors put their hope in you;
they trusted — and you delivered them.
22.5:
They cried to you and were rescued;
they trusted in you and were never disgraced.
22.6:
But I — I am a worm, not even a man:
an object of contempt, despised by the crowds.
22.7:
Everyone who sees me jeers;
they curl their lips and shake their heads:
22.8:
'He claimed the LORD would save him—
let the LORD rescue him now!
Let God deliver him — if he delights in him so much!'
22.9:
Yet you are the one who brought me out of the womb,
who made me trust you at my mother's breast.
22.10:
From the moment of birth I was placed in your care;
from my mother's womb you have been my God.
22.11:
Do not stay far from me —
trouble is at my door
and there is no one else to turn to.
22.12:
A herd of bulls has closed in around me;
the great bulls of Bashan have me encircled;
22.13:
they gape their mouths at me
like lions that tear their prey and roar.
22.14:
I drain out like spilled water;
all my bones come apart at the joints.
My heart has turned to wax —
melting away deep inside me.
22.17:
I can count every bone in my body;
they stare at me — gloating.
22.18:
They divide my clothes among themselves
and throw dice for what I am wearing.
22.19:
But you, LORD — do not keep your distance!
You are my strength; hurry to help me!
22.20:
Save my life from the sword —
my one and only life from the grip of these dogs.
22.21:
Rescue me from the lion's mouth!
From the horns of the wild oxen — you have answered me!
22.22:
I will declare your name to my family —
in the full assembly I will sing your praise.
22.23:
All who fear the LORD — give him praise!
All the seed of Jacob, give him glory!
All the offspring of Israel, stand in awe of him!
22.25:
In the great assembly my praise will rise to you;
I will carry out my vows before all who fear him.
22.27:
Every corner of the earth will remember and return to the LORD;
every family of every nation will bow before him.
22.28:
For the kingdom is the LORD's —
he holds the nations in his hand.
22.30:
Future generations will serve him;
the story of the Lord will be told to those yet to come.
22.31:
A people not yet born will come to hear
that he has brought it all to pass —
he has done it.

23.1:
A Psalm of David.

The LORD is my shepherd —
there is nothing I lack.
23.2:
He lets me rest in meadows of fresh grass;
he leads me to water where I can be still.
23.4:
Even when the path runs through death's dark valley,
I fear nothing —
for you are right beside me.
Your rod and your staff —
they are my comfort.
23.5:
You spread a feast for me
right in front of those who want me gone.
You pour oil on my head;
my cup spills over.
23.6:
Nothing but goodness and steadfast love will track me down
every day that I am alive —
and I will make the LORD's house my home
for all my remaining days.

24.1:
A Psalm of David.

The earth and everything it holds belong to the LORD —
the world and all who live there.
24.2:
For he laid its foundations over the seas
and set it firm above the great rivers.
24.3:
Who may climb the LORD's mountain?
Who may take their stand in his holy place?
24.4:
The one whose hands are clean and whose heart is right —
who has never given himself over to emptiness
or sworn by lies.
24.5:
He will receive the LORD's blessing,
the saving righteousness that comes from God his deliverer.
24.6:
This is the company of those who seek the LORD,
who look for the face of Jacob's God. Selah
24.7:
Lift up your heads, you ancient gates!
Rise up, you doors that have stood since the beginning —
the King of glory is coming in!
24.8:
Who is this King of glory?
The LORD — fierce and mighty,
the LORD — invincible in battle!
24.9:
Lift up your heads, you ancient gates!
Rise up, you ageless doors —
the King of glory is coming in!
24.10:
Who is this King of glory?
The LORD of Armies —
he alone is the King of glory. Selah

31.1:
To the choirmaster. Of David.
In you, LORD, I shelter. Never let me be shamed—
rescue me in your righteousness.
31.2:
Lean down and listen.
Be quick to pull me out.
Be a rock of refuge for me—a fortress that saves.
31.3:
You are my rock and my fortress.
For your own name's sake—lead me, guide me.
31.4:
Tear me free from the net they hid for me—
you are my strength, my only refuge.
31.5:
Into your hands I place my spirit—my very breath, my life.
You have ransomed me, LORD.
You are the God who keeps faith.
31.6:
I turn my back on those who pursue empty lies.
I have staked myself on the LORD.
31.7:
I will be glad—I will sing out—
because your steadfast love holds me.
You saw my misery. You knew the anguish I was carrying.
31.8:
You did not deliver me into enemy hands.
You set my feet in open ground.
31.9:
Have mercy on me, LORD—I am collapsing.
My eyes have gone dim with grief.
My whole self—soul and body—is wasting away.
31.10:
My years have dissolved in grief—
a life measured out in sighs.
My strength gives out because of what I have done wrong.
My bones are going to dust.
31.11:
My enemies see me and sneer.
My neighbors are ashamed of me.
Old friends are frightened—they cross the street to avoid me.
31.12:
I am as forgotten as a corpse.
I have become a smashed pot—no use to anyone,
no longer even remembered.
31.13:
I hear the whisper-campaigns all around me.
Terror on every side.
They are meeting together, planning against me—
all of them scheming to end my life.
31.14:
But I have trusted in you, LORD.
I said it then; I say it now: You are my God.
31.15:
Every moment of my life belongs to you.
Pull me out of their hands—
out of the grip of my enemies and everyone hunting me down.
31.16:
Let your face shine on me, your servant.
Save me—with that steadfast love of yours.
31.17:
Do not let me be shamed, LORD—I have called on you.
Let it be the wicked who carry shame.
Let them go silently into the grave.
31.18:
Silence those lying mouths
that speak against the righteous
with such breathtaking arrogance and contempt.
31.19:
What abundance you have stored away
for those who revere you!
What you have done openly, before every watching eye,
for those who shelter in you—
31.20:
You hide them in the secret place of your own presence—
safe from human plotting.
You shelter them from the wars that words can make.
31.21:
Praise the LORD!
He showed me the wonders of his steadfast love
when I was trapped—a city under siege.
31.22:
In my panic I said, 'I am completely cut off—beyond your sight.'
But you heard me.
You heard my desperate plea.
31.23:
Love the LORD, all his faithful ones!
He protects everyone who is loyal to him—
and the arrogant get paid back in full, not a shekel short.
31.24:
Be strong. Let your heart take courage.
All of you who have placed your hope in the LORD.

32.1:
A Maskil—of David.
Blessed is the person whose rebellion has been lifted away—
whose sin God has covered over.
32.2:
Blessed is the one the LORD does not hold accountable for wrongdoing—
who has also stopped deceiving themselves.
32.3:
When I said nothing—kept it locked inside—
my bones deteriorated.
All day long I groaned.
32.4:
Day and night your hand pressed down hard on me.
My vitality evaporated—as in a scorching summer.
Selah.
32.5:
I stopped hiding.
I laid my sin open before you.
I said: I am going to confess what I have done against you, LORD—
and the moment I did, you lifted the guilt of it all.
Selah.
32.6:
So let every person who is faithful pray to you while there is still time—
before the floodwaters rise.
When the great surge comes, it will not reach them.
32.7:
You are where I hide.
You shield me from danger.
You encircle me with cries of rescue.
Selah.
32.8:
I will teach you.
I will show you the road you should take.
My eye will be on you as I guide you.
32.9:
Don't be a horse or mule—animals with no understanding
who have to be forced into line with bit and bridle.
Don't make God drag you.
32.10:
The wicked carry a heavy weight of their own sorrows.
But those who trust in the LORD are encircled by steadfast love.
32.11:
Celebrate! Sing out!
You who live right before God—let the shout rise from you.
All of you with honest hearts—raise the noise.

33.1:
Sing out to the LORD, you righteous ones—
praise sits beautifully on those who live right.
33.2:
Play the lyre and give thanks.
Pull music from the ten-stringed harp and praise him.
33.3:
Sing him something new.
Play with all the craft you have—
and let the shout of joy go out.
33.4:
The LORD's word is straight and clean.
Everything he does is done with perfect faithfulness.
33.5:
He is in love with righteousness and justice.
The whole earth is saturated with his steadfast love.
33.6:
God spoke—and the heavens came into being.
His breath, the breath of his mouth, brought every star into existence.
This is the same speech that began all things in Genesis.
33.7:
He heaps the oceans up like something stacked and measured.
He stores the depths as though in a treasury.
33.8:
Let every creature on earth hold the LORD in reverence.
Let every human being stand in awe of him.
33.9:
He spoke—and it was.
He commanded—and it held.
33.10:
The LORD dismantles the strategies of nations.
Every people's long-laid plan—he foils it.
33.11:
But the LORD's own counsel stands—unshaken, permanent.
What he purposes in his heart holds across every generation.
33.12:
Happy is the nation whose God is the LORD—
the people he has chosen to be his own possession.
33.13:
From heaven the LORD looks down—
he sees every human being.
33.14:
From where he sits, he looks out
over everyone living on this planet.
33.15:
He is the one who formed each heart—
and he pays attention to everything each person does.
33.16:
No king survives by the size of his army.
No warrior escapes by sheer physical power.
33.17:
A war-horse is an empty promise of safety.
All its power will not save you.
33.18:
But look: the LORD's eye is fixed on those who revere him—
on those who have placed their hope in his steadfast love.
33.19:
He watches to rescue them from death—
to keep them breathing even when the land starves.
33.20:
We are waiting for the LORD.
He is our help and our shield—we have nothing else.
33.21:
Our hearts are full of joy in him—
because we have staked ourselves on his holy name.
33.22:
LORD, let your steadfast love rest on us—
the same measure as the hope we have placed in you.

25.1:
Of David.
To you, LORD—I raise my whole self up.
25.2:
My God, I have put my trust in you.
Do not let me be disgraced.
Do not let my enemies stand over me in triumph.
25.3:
No one who waits for you ends in disgrace.
Shame is reserved for those who betray without cause.
25.4:
Show me how you move through the world, LORD.
Teach me the routes you travel.
25.5:
Guide me in your faithfulness—teach me its paths.
You are my saving God.
All day long I am waiting—and only for you.
25.6:
Remember your compassion, LORD, and your steadfast love—
these have been your character from before time remembers.
25.7:
Do not remember what I was when I was young—
the rebellions, the failures.
Remember me instead through the lens of your steadfast love,
because of who you are, LORD.
25.8:
The LORD is good and straight—and precisely because of that,
he teaches those who have gone wrong how to find the road again.
25.9:
The lowly, the teachable—
he leads them in the right direction
and shows them personally how he moves.
25.10:
Every road the LORD walks is paved with steadfast love and faithfulness—
and this is the lived experience of everyone who keeps his covenant.
25.11:
For the honor of your own name, LORD—forgive me.
My guilt is heavy. You know how great it is.
25.12:
Who is the person who truly reveres the LORD?
God will personally show them which road to take.
25.13:
Their life will rest in good things,
and their descendants will have the land as their own.
25.14:
The LORD lets those who revere him into his inner counsel—
he shares the secret of his covenant with them.
25.15:
My eyes are fixed on the LORD—only him.
He is the one who will pull my feet free of whatever snare is set.
25.16:
Turn toward me. Show me your mercy.
I am alone out here—and I am suffering.
25.17:
The anguish inside me has been growing.
Bring me out of the tight place I am in.
25.18:
See what I am carrying—the suffering, the hardship.
And forgive every sin that brought me here.
25.19:
Look at how many there are—the ones set against me.
Their hatred is not mild. It is violent and relentless.
25.20:
Keep my life safe—bring me through.
Do not let me be humiliated.
You are where I have taken cover.
25.21:
Let my whole life—its honesty, its uprightness—stand as my defense,
while I wait for you.
25.22:
God—redeem Israel.
Bring your whole people out of every trouble they carry.

26.1:
Of David.
Declare me innocent, LORD—I ask it.
I have walked a straight road; I have trusted you without turning aside.
26.2:
Test me, LORD. Run the examination.
Look into my heart and into my deepest self.
26.3:
Your steadfast love has been constantly in view for me.
I have walked by the light of your faithfulness.
26.4:
I have not made my home among liars.
I do not keep company with those who wear a mask.
26.5:
The gathering of evil people—I want nothing to do with it.
I will not take my seat among the wicked.
26.6:
I wash my hands in innocence
and take my place beside your altar, LORD—
26.7:
my voice lifted in praise,
and the story of everything you have done pouring out of me.
26.8:
LORD, I love where you live—
this house, and especially the place where your glory makes its home.
26.9:
Do not sweep me away with people who are headed for ruin—
with those whose hands are soaked in violence.
26.10:
Their hands run with evil schemes.
Their right hands clutch the money that bought their verdicts.
26.11:
My own road is different: I walk with a whole heart.
Buy me back from danger, LORD. Be merciful.
26.12:
My feet are on solid, level ground.
In the full assembly of the people I will bless the LORD.

27.1:
Of David.
The LORD is my light—he is my rescue.
Who is there left to fear?
The LORD is the fortified wall around my life.
Who has any power to terrify me?
27.2:
When my enemies advanced to tear me apart—
adversaries, hostile forces pressing in—
they tripped over themselves and went down.
27.3:
Let an entire army set up camp against me—
my heart is not afraid.
Let war itself break out at my door—
I am still sure of this.
27.4:
One thing—I have asked it, and I keep asking—
that I might live inside the LORD's presence
every remaining day of my life:
to look at his beauty,
to meet with him in his temple.
27.5:
Because when trouble comes he will tuck me inside his own home.
He will hide me in the deepest recess of his tent.
He will lift me up and set me on a rock too high for trouble to reach.
27.6:
Even now my head rises above the enemies encircling me.
In his tent I will bring offerings—shouts of joy going up with the smoke.
I will sing. I will play music for the LORD.
27.7:
LORD—hear me. I am calling out.
Show me your grace. Answer.
27.8:
When you said, 'Come and find me'—
my heart immediately answered:
'I am looking for your face, LORD. Only your face.'
27.9:
Do not turn your face away from me.
Do not send your servant away in anger—you, who have always been my help.
Do not drop me. Do not walk away.
You are the God who saves me.
27.10:
Even if my own father and mother should let me go—
the LORD gathers me in.
27.11:
Teach me your way, LORD.
Lead me on the level road—my enemies are watching every misstep I make.
27.12:
Do not hand me over to what my enemies want.
False witnesses have stood up against me—
they breathe out violence with every word they say.
27.13:
I would have collapsed—except that I believe this:
I will see the goodness of the LORD here,
in this present life, while I am still among the living.
27.14:
Wait for the LORD.
Dig in. Let courage take root in your heart.
Wait—keep waiting—for the LORD.

28.1:
Of David.
I am calling out to you, LORD.
My Rock—do not go quiet on me.
If you say nothing, I am no better than those already sinking into the grave.
28.2:
Hear what I am asking for—mercy—
as I reach my hands out in prayer toward the innermost place of your house.
28.3:
Do not sweep me away with those who are wicked—
with people who carry smooth words on their lips
while their hearts are scheming harm.
28.4:
Let them receive exactly what they have given.
Match their deeds—returned in full.
Give them the wages their work has earned.
28.5:
They never once noticed what God was doing in the world around them—
never saw his hand at work.
So he will dismantle them. And not rebuild.
28.6:
Let the LORD be praised!
He heard what I was asking him for.
28.7:
The LORD is my strength—my shield.
I trusted my heart to him—and I was rescued.
My heart is leaping now.
I am singing my thanks.
28.8:
The LORD is strength to his whole people—
the fortress of safety for the one he has anointed.
28.9:
Save your people, LORD. Bless what belongs to you.
Be their shepherd. Carry them.
Do it forever.

29.1:
Of David.
You powers of the heavenly court—give to the LORD!
Give him glory. Give him the strength that is his.
29.2:
Render to the LORD the honor that belongs to his name.
Worship him—prostrate in the blazing splendor of his holiness.
29.3:
Now his voice rolls over the waters.
The God of glory—thundering—
the LORD over oceans without count.
29.4:
His voice: pure power.
His voice: breathtaking majesty.
29.5:
His voice splits the great cedars—
the LORD tears apart even the cedars of Lebanon.
29.6:
Lebanon bucks like a calf at the sound.
Sirion leaps like a young bull.
29.8:
His voice convulses the desert—
even the wilderness of Kadesh shudders at the sound.
29.9:
His voice sends the deer into sudden labor—
strips the forest down to bare wood.
And in his temple, every voice: Glory!
29.10:
The LORD sat above the primordial Flood—sovereign over the greatest chaos the world has ever seen.
The LORD sits now, as he always has, enthroned as King forever.
29.11:
The LORD gives his people the same strength he displayed in the storm.
He blesses them—with peace.

30.1:
A Song for the Dedication of the House—of David.
I will lift you high, LORD—because you lifted me.
You did not let my enemies stand over me and celebrate.
30.2:
LORD, my God—I cried out to you,
and you healed me.
30.3:
You pulled my whole self back from Sheol, LORD—
back from the very edge of the grave.
30.4:
Sing praise to the LORD, all who belong to him.
Let his holy name hear your gratitude.
30.5:
His anger lasts only a moment—but his favor runs the whole length of a life.
Tears may move in for the night—
but by morning: singing.
30.6:
In my comfortable days I said to myself:
'Nothing will ever move me from this.'
30.7:
You, LORD, by your sheer goodwill, had made my life as solid as a mountain.
Then you turned your face away—
and I fell to pieces.
30.8:
So I called out to you, LORD—to you, my Master.
I laid out my case:
30.9:
'What exactly do you gain from my death?
If I go down into the grave, can dust lift its voice to you?
Can dust testify to your faithfulness?'
30.10:
Hear me, LORD.
Be merciful to me.
Be the one who helps me.
30.11:
And you did it—you turned my mourning into dancing.
You took the mourning-cloth off me
and wrapped me in gladness.
30.12:
So that everything in me—my whole self—might sing to you without stopping.
LORD, my God:
I will thank you forever.

34.2:
In the LORD my whole self will boast;
let the humble hear it and rejoice.
34.3:
Come, make the LORD great with me;
let us lift his name higher together.
34.4:
I sought the LORD, and he answered;
from every fear that gripped me he set me free.
34.5:
All who look to him become radiant;
their faces will never be covered with shame.
34.6:
Here — one man in poverty cried out,
and the LORD heard him
and rescued him from every trouble.
34.7:
The angel of the LORD stations himself
around those who fear the LORD
and brings them to safety.
34.8:
Come and taste — find out for yourself that the LORD is good.
Blessed is the one who shelters in him.
34.9:
Fear the LORD, all you who belong to him —
those who fear him go without nothing.
34.10:
Young lions may prowl hungry,
but those who seek the LORD want for nothing good.
34.11:
Come, my children — listen to me;
I will teach you what it means to fear the LORD.
34.12:
Who here wants a full life —
longs for many days stretched out to see what is good?
34.13:
Guard your tongue from what causes harm;
keep your lips from every form of deception.
34.14:
Turn your back on evil — do what is right;
hunt down peace and run after it.
34.15:
The LORD's eyes are fixed on the righteous;
his ears are tuned to every cry they raise.
34.16:
The LORD sets his face against evildoers
to wipe every trace of them from the earth.
34.17:
The righteous cry out — the LORD hears;
from every trouble he brings them out.
34.18:
The LORD stays close to those whose hearts are broken;
he rescues those whose spirit has been ground to dust.
34.19:
The righteous face trouble after trouble —
but the LORD delivers them from every one.
34.20:
He guards every bone in his body —
not one of them will be broken.
34.21:
Evil will be the undoing of the wicked;
those who hate the righteous will face their own condemnation.
34.22:
The LORD ransoms the lives of those who serve him;
not one who shelters in him will ever be condemned.

35.2:
Grip your shield and buckler;
rise up to fight for me.
35.3:
Raise your spear — cut off the path of those who hunt me;
speak this word to my deepest self: 'I am the one who saves you.'
35.4:
Let those who pursue my life be driven back in shame;
let all who scheme to harm me reel in humiliation.
35.5:
Drive them like chaff before the wind;
let the angel of the LORD scatter them.
35.6:
Make their road dark and ice-slick;
send the angel of the LORD to hound them without rest.
35.7:
They laid a hidden net to catch me — unprovoked;
they dug a pit to take my life — for no reason at all.
35.9:
Then my whole self will burst into joy before the LORD,
exulting in the rescue he brings.
35.10:
Every bone I have will shout:
'LORD — who can compare to you?
You rescue the helpless from those who crush them;
the poor and vulnerable from those who strip them bare.'
35.11:
Hostile witnesses surge to their feet;
they interrogate me about things I never did.
35.12:
Good they return with evil —
and my soul is left utterly alone.
35.13:
But when they were sick I put on sackcloth;
I bowed myself low with fasting.
My prayer curved back upon my own breast —
and still I kept on praying.
35.14:
I moved among them as one weeping for a close friend, a brother;
I was bowed down in grief as though mourning my own mother.
35.16:
Like sneering gluttons who mock the sacred,
they bared their teeth at me.
35.18:
I will thank you in the great congregation;
before the vast throng I will sing your praise.
35.20:
They have no intention of making peace —
they brew up lies against those who only want to live undisturbed.
35.21:
They gape their mouths wide at me:
'Aha! Aha! — we saw it ourselves, with our own eyes!'
35.22:
You have seen it, LORD — do not keep silent!
Do not stay far away, my Lord.
35.23:
Rouse yourself — stir yourself awake, Lord!
Defend my case; it is your cause too,
my God and my Lord.
35.24:
Give me the justice your righteousness demands, LORD my God;
do not hand them the victory over me.
35.25:
Do not let them say in their hearts: 'Exactly what we wanted!'
Do not let them say: 'We swallowed him whole!'
35.26:
Let everyone who celebrates my ruin
be covered in shame and left in confusion.
Let those who puff themselves up against me
be wrapped in disgrace like a garment.
35.27:
But let those who want justice for me
shout for joy and be glad,
and say without stopping:
'The LORD is great — he takes delight
in the well-being of the one who serves him!'
35.28:
Then my tongue will tell of your righteousness
and sing your praise all day long.

36.1:
To the worship leader. A Psalm of David, servant of the LORD.

Transgression delivers its oracle to the wicked
from within his own heart:
there is no fear of God behind his eyes.
36.2:
He has convinced himself his guilt will never come to light —
that it cannot be exposed and despised.
36.5:
Your steadfast love, LORD, spans the height of the heavens;
your faithfulness rises to the clouds.
36.7:
How unspeakably precious is your steadfast love, O God!
All who are human run to shelter
under the shadow of your wings.
36.8:
They feast on the abundance of your house;
you give them to drink from the river of your own delights.
36.9:
With you is the source of all life;
by your light we finally see everything clearly.
36.10:
Keep on showing your steadfast love to those who know you;
stretch your righteousness over the straight-hearted.
36.11:
Do not let the arrogant trample over me;
do not let the hand of the wicked push me out.

37.1:
Of David.
Do not burn with resentment over the wicked.
Do not envy those who do harm.
37.2:
They are grass. Cut down, and soon.
Like green growth in the heat—they will wither.
37.3:
Trust the LORD—and act on it.
Live in the land. Feed on what faithfulness provides.
37.4:
Take your deepest pleasure in the LORD himself—
and he will give you everything your heart has been asking for.
37.5:
Roll your burden of a path onto the LORD.
Trust him—and watch him move.
37.6:
He will make your righteousness break through like sunrise—
your vindication blazing at midday.
37.7:
Be still before the LORD.
Wait for him—quietly, without straining.
Do not burn with resentment over the person who seems to get everything they want,
the one whose wicked schemes keep working.
37.8:
Let anger go.
Abandon wrath.
Stop fuming—it only takes you somewhere you do not want to go.
37.9:
The wicked will be cut off—every one of them.
But those who wait for the LORD will possess the earth.
37.10:
A short while more—and the wicked simply vanish.
You will look for them where they were.
Nothing.
37.11:
The meek will inherit the earth—not the aggressive, not the successful schemers, but the meek.
And they will feast on a peace that has no limit.
[Jesus lifts this promise directly into the Sermon on the Mount: Matthew 5:5.]
37.12:
The wicked brood over plots against the righteous.
They grind their teeth in hatred.
37.13:
The Lord laughs—that deep, unhurried laugh—
because he already sees the day that is coming for them.
37.14:
The wicked draw their swords and string their bows—
their target: the poor, the helpless,
those who do nothing but live right.
37.15:
Their swords will go straight into their own hearts.
Every bow they drew will snap.
37.16:
A righteous person's little is worth more
than the stacked fortunes of any number of wicked.
37.17:
The power the wicked rely on will be broken.
But the LORD holds the righteous up.
37.18:
The LORD has his eye on every day of those who live with integrity.
What he is keeping for them will never end.
37.19:
When trouble comes—when things are at their worst—they will not be disgraced.
When famine comes, they will still have enough.
37.20:
The wicked will perish.
The LORD's enemies, for all their apparent glory,
will fade like pasture grass in summer heat—
burned off, gone, vanishing into smoke.
37.21:
The wicked borrows and never repays.
The righteous gives freely—that is simply their character.
37.22:
The LORD's blessing carries the inheritance of the earth.
The LORD's curse carries being cut off entirely.
37.23:
The LORD is the one who makes a person's steps firm.
He takes pleasure in watching that life unfold.
37.24:
If he stumbles, he will not crash—
because the LORD has his hand.
37.25:
I speak from long experience:
I have never seen God abandon a righteous person—
never seen their children having to beg for food.
37.26:
The righteous person is always giving—always lending what they can.
Their children are a blessing to everyone around them.
37.27:
Turn away from evil.
Do good instead.
That is the path to remaining—permanently—where God has placed you.
37.28:
The LORD loves justice—that is a settled truth.
He will not abandon those who are faithful to him.
He keeps them—always.
But the children of the wicked inherit nothing but the cut-off.
37.29:
The righteous will receive the earth as their home—
and they will live in it, permanently.
37.30:
Wisdom comes out of the righteous person's mouth.
Every word they say leans toward what is right.
37.31:
God's instruction lives deep in this person's heart.
That is why they do not stumble.
37.32:
The wicked stake out the righteous—
they want them dead.
37.33:
The LORD will not hand them over.
When the trial comes, he will not let them be condemned.
37.34:
Wait for the LORD.
Keep to his way.
He will lift you up to receive the earth—
and you will be there to see the wicked eliminated.
37.35:
I have watched the wicked—powerful, ruthless—
spread out like a lush tree in rich soil,
looking as though they have always been here and always will be.
37.36:
And then he was simply gone.
I looked where he had been.
Nothing.
37.37:
Watch the blameless person.
Notice what those who live uprightly look like.
The future they are walking toward is peace.
37.38:
But the rebels—every one of them—will be destroyed together.
The wicked have no future. It has already been cut off.
37.39:
When the righteous need rescuing, the LORD is the one who rescues them.
He is their fortress when trouble comes.
37.40:
The LORD is their help.
He pulls them free—again and again—from the grip of the wicked.
He saves them.
All because they made him their refuge.

38.1:
Of David. Offered as a memorial before God.
LORD, do not correct me while burning with anger.
Do not discipline me in the heat of your fury.
[This opening echoes Psalm 6:1 exactly — two penitential psalms that share an entry wound.]
38.2:
Your arrows are sunk deep into me.
Your hand is pressing down, and it does not lift.
38.3:
There is nothing sound in my body—your anger has seen to that.
My very bones have no peace—because of what I have done.
38.4:
My wrongdoings have risen over my head like floodwater.
They are a weight I cannot lift—
heavy beyond endurance.
38.5:
My wounds have gone rotten—putrid—
because of my own foolishness.
38.6:
I walk bent over, hunched to the ground.
All day long I move through life in mourning.
38.7:
My loins burn with inflammation.
There is not one sound part in me.
38.8:
I am numb and broken.
The groaning comes from somewhere deeper than I can reach—
the turmoil of a heart that cannot settle.
38.9:
Lord, you see it all.
Every aching desire I have is open before you.
My sighs are not hidden.
38.10:
My heart pounds wildly.
My strength is gone.
The very light in my eyes has dimmed and left.
38.11:
The people who love me stay away from where I am sick.
Friends, companions, close family—
they all keep their distance.
38.12:
My enemies have set traps for me—
they are after my life.
All day long they whisper ruin and rehearse their deceptions.
38.13:
But I say nothing.
I am a deaf man who hears nothing,
a mute who does not open his mouth.
38.14:
I have become someone who does not even argue back—
not a word of rebuttal in my mouth.
38.15:
But my hope is in you, LORD—only you.
You will answer me, Lord, my God.
38.16:
I kept thinking: Do not let them celebrate my collapse—
those who are already puffing themselves up over me,
waiting for my foot to slip.
38.17:
I am on the verge of collapse.
The pain does not leave.
38.18:
I am going to say it plainly: this is my wrongdoing.
I am undone by what I have done.
[This is the psalm's hinge — confession at the centre, the movement Ps 51 will map at full scale.]
38.19:
Meanwhile my enemies are vigorous—thriving.
The ones who hate me for no reason keep growing in number.
38.20:
They repay my goodness with evil—they accuse me
precisely because I have been pursuing what is right.
38.21:
Do not leave me, LORD.
My God—please do not stay distant from me.
38.22:
Hurry to me, Lord—
you are my only salvation.

39.1:
To the choirmaster. For Jeduthun. Of David.
I resolved: I will watch my step so my tongue won't betray me into sin.
I will keep my mouth under guard—especially while the wicked are watching.
39.2:
I went completely mute.
I held back—even from saying good things.
But the pain only grew worse.
39.3:
My heart smoldered while I brooded.
The more I turned it over, the hotter it burned—
until at last I had to speak.
39.4:
'Tell me, LORD—what is my end?
How many days have I got?
Let me understand just how thin my existence really is.'
39.5:
You have made my days no longer than a handspan.
Before you, my whole lifetime adds up to nothing.
At their very best, every human being is only a breath—a vapor.
Selah.
39.6:
Every person walks around like a shadow—
a silhouette, no real substance.
They churn in frantic activity—all empty noise.
They hoard and accumulate and die not knowing who will inherit it all.
39.7:
So then—what am I actually waiting for, Lord?
You. Only you. That is where my hope stands.
39.8:
Rescue me from all my rebellions against you.
Don't let me become the target of mockery from those who have no wisdom.
39.9:
I am silent.
I won't open my mouth.
You have done all of this—I know it.
39.10:
Lift your blow from me.
I am being destroyed under the weight of your hand.
39.11:
When you rebuke and discipline a person for their sin,
you eat away everything they prize—like a moth destroying fine cloth.
In the end every human being is only vapor.
Selah.
39.12:
Hear my prayer, LORD.
Listen to my cry.
Don't be silent while I weep.
I am a stranger here—a temporary resident in your world—
just as all my ancestors were before me.
39.13:
Let up—just long enough
that I can breathe again, maybe even smile,
before I leave this world and am no more.

40.1:
To the choirmaster. Of David.
I waited—deliberately, persistently—for the LORD.
And he bent down and heard my cry.
40.2:
He reached into the horrible pit—the sucking mud—and lifted me out.
He planted my feet on solid rock.
He made my footing sure.
40.3:
He gave me a new song—
praise rising to our God.
Many people will watch and be struck with awe,
and they will trust in the LORD.
40.4:
Blessed is the person who has staked everything on the LORD—
who hasn't gone chasing after the arrogant
or turned aside toward those who follow empty lies.
40.5:
How vast your wonders are, LORD my God—
how vast your thoughts toward us!
Nothing and no one equals you.
If I tried to name them all—to speak each one—
there are more than I could ever number.
40.6:
You don't want animal sacrifice or grain-offerings.
You have opened my ears—bored them open for obedience, like a servant ready to hear.
Burnt offerings and sin offerings are not what you require.
[The Greek translation rendered this verse as 'a body you have prepared for me'—the form quoted in Hebrews 10:5–7 about Christ's embodied obedience. The Hebrew speaks of ears: the organ of hearing and loyal service.]
40.7:
'Here I am,' I said. 'I come—
and the book's scroll says I would.'
40.8:
I am delighted to do what you want, my God.
Your teaching is written on the inside of me.
40.9:
I proclaimed your righteousness in the great assembly—out loud, before everyone.
I did not close my lips.
You know that, LORD.
40.10:
Your righteousness—I didn't lock it away inside me.
Your faithfulness and salvation—I proclaimed them.
Your steadfast love and your truth—I announced them
before the full hearing of the great assembly.
Nothing held back, nothing concealed.
40.11:
For your part, LORD—do not hold back your tender compassion from me.
Let your steadfast love and truth be my constant guard.
40.12:
Evils beyond counting have closed in around me.
My own iniquities have caught up with me—I can't see through them.
More than the hairs on my head.
My heart is sinking.
40.13:
LORD—please—deliver me.
Do not delay in coming.
40.14:
Let everyone hunting me down be shamed and confounded.
Let those who want me destroyed be turned back in dishonor.
40.15:
Those who taunt me with 'Aha! Aha!'—
let the shame fall back on them instead.
40.16:
But let everyone who is seeking you burst into joy and gladness—in you.
Let those who love your rescue never stop saying:
'The LORD is great!'
40.17:
I have nothing and I know it.
But the Lord has me in mind.
You are my help; you are my rescuer.
My God—please don't be slow.

41.1:
To the choirmaster. Of David.
Blessed is the person who notices the weak and takes them seriously—
the LORD will deliver that person when trouble comes.
41.2:
The LORD guards that person and keeps them alive.
They are regarded as blessed throughout the land.
You will not let their enemies have their way with them.
41.3:
When sickness has them bedridden, the LORD is there beside them.
In their illness you bring restoration.
41.4:
I myself said this:
'LORD, have mercy on me.
Heal me—I have sinned against you.'
41.5:
My enemies say the worst about me behind my back:
'When does he finally die? When does his name disappear for good?'
41.6:
When one of them comes to visit—a pretend visit—
his words mean nothing while his mind notes everything it can use against me.
Then he walks out and tells the whole story.
41.7:
Everyone who hates me huddles together and whispers.
They are all scheming my ruin.
41.8:
They are saying: 'Something has broken in him that cannot be fixed.
He is down—he will never get up from this.'
41.9:
Even the person I trusted most—the one who broke bread at my table—
has raised his heel against me.
Betrayal from inside the circle.
41.10:
But you, LORD—have mercy on me.
Raise me up so that I can pay them back.
41.11:
I know you favor me by this single fact:
my enemy has not broken out in triumph over me.
41.12:
And me—you are holding me steady in my integrity.
You have set me before your face. Forever.
41.13:
Blessed be the LORD, the God of Israel—
from everlasting to everlasting.
Amen. And Amen.

42.1:
To the choirmaster. A Maskil of the Sons of Korah.
As a deer—desperate for the water-brook—
so my soul is desperate for you, God.
42.2:
My whole self is parched—thirsting for God, the God who is alive and real.
When will I get to come and stand before him?
42.4:
I pour out my soul in memory:
how I used to walk at the head of the crowd,
leading the procession up to God's house—
the shouts of joy, the songs of thanksgiving,
the whole multitude keeping festival together.
42.5:
Why are you sinking, my soul?
Why this churning turmoil inside me?
Hope in God.
I will praise him yet—he is my rescue, he is my God.
42.6:
My soul has collapsed.
From here—from the Jordan valley, the Hermon ridges, from little Mount Mizar—
I reach back across the distance to remember you.
42.7:
The deep is calling out to the deep—
at the crash of your waterspouts.
Wave after wave, breaker after breaker—
all of them rolling over me.
[Tehom—the primordial deep of Genesis 1—speaks the language of overwhelming chaos, reversed creation.]
42.8:
And yet—by day the LORD pours out his steadfast love.
By night his song rises in me: a prayer to the God who gives me life.
42.9:
I say to God—the Rock I stand on—
'Why have you forgotten me?
Why am I walking around in this grief
while the enemy crushes me?'
42.10:
It is like a blade breaking through my bones—
my enemies mocking me every single day:
'Where is this God of yours?'
42.11:
Why are you still sinking, my soul?
Why this inner turmoil that won't stop?
Hope in God.
I will praise him yet.
He is the health of my face—he is my God.

43.2:
You are the God who is my fortress — so why have you turned your back on me?
Why do I walk in mourning
while my enemy presses down on me?
43.3:
Send out your light, send out your faithfulness —
let them lead me, guide me
to your holy mountain, to the place where you dwell.
43.4:
Then I will come to the altar of God —
to God, the source of my deepest joy —
and I will praise you with the harp,
O God, my God.
43.5:
Why are you so downcast, my soul?
Why this turmoil churning inside me?
Put your hope in God —
I will yet praise him again,
the one who saves me and is my God.

44.1:
To the worship leader. A Maskil of the sons of Korah.

O God, we have heard it with our own ears —
our ancestors told us
what you did in their time,
in those days long before us.
44.2:
With your own hand you drove out nations
and planted our people in their place;
you shattered peoples
and thrust them out.
44.3:
It was not their swords that gave them the land;
it was not their own strength that saved them.
It was your right hand, your arm,
the light of your face —
because you were pleased with them.
44.4:
You are my King, O God;
decree deliverance for Jacob.
44.5:
Through you we drive back our enemies;
by your name we trample those who rise against us.
44.6:
I do not put my confidence in my bow;
my sword cannot deliver me.
44.7:
But you — you are the one who saves us from our enemies
and covers those who hate us with shame.
44.9:
But now — you have thrown us away, covered us in disgrace;
you no longer march out with our armies.
44.10:
You have made us turn tail before the enemy;
those who hate us have stripped us bare.
44.11:
You handed us over like sheep destined for slaughter;
you scattered us among the nations.
44.12:
You sold your own people for nothing —
you gained not a thing from the transaction.
44.13:
You have made us the butt of jokes among our neighbors —
scorn and derision to everyone nearby.
44.14:
We have become a proverb of misfortune among the nations;
the peoples shake their heads at us.
44.15:
My humiliation is before me all day long;
shame has covered my face.
44.16:
The mocker's voice rings in my ears —
the enemy who wants revenge fills my world with shame.
44.17:
All this has fallen on us — and yet we have not forgotten you;
we have not been disloyal to your covenant.
44.18:
Our heart did not draw back;
our feet did not wander from your path.
44.19:
Yet you crushed us in a desert haunt of jackals
and wrapped us in deathly darkness.
44.20:
If we had forgotten the name of our God,
or spread our hands in prayer toward a foreign god —
44.21:
Would God not have found it out?
He knows every secret the heart conceals.
44.22:
But for your sake we face death all day long;
we are counted as sheep marked for slaughter.
44.23:
Wake up, Lord — why are you sleeping?
Rise up! Do not abandon us forever!
44.24:
Why do you turn your face away?
Why have you forgotten our suffering and our misery?
44.25:
We are pressed flat — our very self ground into the dust;
our bodies cling to the earth.
44.26:
Rise up — come and help us!
For the sake of your steadfast love, redeem us.

45.1:
To the worship leader. To the tune of 'The Lilies.' For the sons of Korah. A Maskil, a Love Song.

My heart is overflowing with a beautiful theme —
I address my words to the king.
My tongue moves like the pen
of a masterful writer.
45.3:
Strap your sword to your thigh, O champion —
clothe yourself in glory and magnificence.
45.4:
Ride out in your splendor and conquer —
championing truth, humility, and justice.
Let your right hand do great and fearsome things.
45.6:
Your throne, O God, stands forever and ever;
the scepter of your kingdom is a scepter of justice.
45.7:
You have loved what is right and hated what is wrong —
so God, your own God, has anointed you
with the oil of celebration,
exalting you above all who stand beside you.
45.8:
Myrrh, aloe, and cassia — your robes carry their fragrance;
from within the ivory halls, music has filled your heart with joy.
45.10:
Listen, daughter — pay close attention;
lean in with your ear:
leave your people behind,
forget your father's house.
45.11:
The king desires your beauty —
he is your lord; honor him.
45.12:
Even the daughter of Tyre brings gifts;
the wealthiest of peoples come seeking your grace.
45.13:
The daughter of the king — glorious within;
her gown is woven through with gold.
45.15:
With joy and exultation they come in;
they enter the halls of the king.
45.16:
Where your ancestors were, your sons will stand;
you will set them as princes across the whole earth.
45.17:
I will make your name live on in every generation;
therefore the nations will praise you forever and ever.

46.1:
To the worship leader. For the sons of Korah. To Alamoth. A Song.

God is our refuge and our strength —
a help that is always ready in the moment of trouble.
46.2:
Therefore we will not be afraid —
not even if the earth itself collapses,
not even if the mountains plunge into the heart of the sea.
46.3:
Though the waters rage and foam,
though mountains tremble at the rising tide —
Selah.
46.4:
But there is a river —
its streams bringing gladness to the city of God,
the holy place where the Most High makes his home.
46.6:
Nations roared, kingdoms crumbled —
he raised his voice
and the earth melted away.
46.8:
Come — look at what the LORD has done;
see what devastation he has laid across the earth.
46.9:
He brings war to its end at the edges of the earth —
snapping bows, splintering spears,
burning every chariot to ash.

47.2:
For the LORD Most High is awesome —
a great King over all the earth.
47.3:
He subdues nations beneath us
and peoples under our feet.
47.4:
He chose our inheritance for us —
the glory given to Jacob, whom he loved.
Selah.
47.5:
God has ascended to his throne —
to shouts of joy, to the blast of the shofar.
47.6:
Sing praise to God — sing praise!
Sing praise to our King — sing praise!
47.7:
For God is King over all the earth;
let your praise be thoughtful and full of meaning.
47.8:
God reigns over the nations —
God is seated on his holy throne.
47.9:
The princes of the nations have gathered
to become the people of the God of Abraham.
The rulers of the earth belong to God —
he is lifted high above them all.

48.1:
A Song. A Psalm of the sons of Korah.

Great is the LORD, worthy of all praise,
in the city of our God, on his holy mountain.
48.2:
Mount Zion — beautiful in its height,
the joy of all the earth.
From its northern heights it stands:
the city of the great King.
48.3:
In her towers and citadels
God has revealed himself as a sure refuge.
48.4:
Look — the kings of the earth joined forces;
they came advancing together.
48.5:
They saw — and stopped cold;
they were thrown into terror and fled.
48.6:
Trembling seized them on the spot —
anguish like a woman in the grip of labor.
48.7:
With an east wind you scatter
the great ships of Tarshish.
48.8:
What we had only heard, now we have seen:
here in the city of the LORD of hosts,
here in the city of our God —
God keeps it safe forever.
Selah.
48.9:
In the heart of your temple, O God,
we have dwelt on your steadfast love.
48.10:
As great as your name is, O God,
so great is your praise — stretching to the farthest edge of the earth.
Your right hand overflows with righteousness.
48.12:
Walk all around Zion — encircle her;
count her towers.
48.13:
Study her walls carefully, walk through her citadels —
so that you can tell the coming generation.
48.14:
This is what God is — our God forever and ever.
He will lead us even through death.

55.1:
To the choirmaster. With stringed instruments. A Maskil of David.
Hear my prayer, God.
Don't turn away from what I'm begging you for.
55.2:
Turn toward me—answer me.
I am churning inside this lament, moaning out loud—
55.3:
—because the enemy's voice is ringing in my ears,
because the wicked are bearing down on me.
They are loading me with trouble,
and their anger against me is personal and fierce.
55.4:
My heart is in agony.
The terrors of death have ambushed me.
55.5:
Fear.
Trembling.
Horror—
piling over me like a wave.
55.6:
'If only I had wings—dove's wings—
I would fly away and settle somewhere quiet.'
55.7:
I would put great distance between myself and all of this.
I would find my rest in the wilderness.
Selah.
55.8:
I would run—
to get out of this battering wind,
out of this tempest.
55.9:
Lord, scatter them.
Mix up their communications—divide their tongues.
I have seen what fills this city: violence, fighting.
55.10:
Day and night they circle the walls.
And inside—iniquity, misery, nothing but trouble.
55.11:
Ruin is camped in the heart of the city.
Oppression and fraud have permanent addresses there.
They never leave.
55.12:
If it were an open enemy doing this,
I could take it.
If it were someone who hated me raising himself against me,
I could find somewhere to hide.
55.13:
But it's you.
You—my equal.
My companion.
The one I walked with, spoke with, trusted.
55.14:
We talked together—real conversation, the kind that goes deep.
We walked side by side in the crowd up to the house of God.
55.15:
Let death steal over them.
Let them go down to Sheol—alive.
For they are soaked through with evil:
in their homes and in their hearts.
55.16:
As for me—I call to God.
The LORD will save me.
55.17:
Evening, morning, and noon—
I pour out my complaint, I moan aloud.
And he hears me.
55.18:
He delivers me unharmed from this war I'm in.
They outnumber me—but he brings me through in peace.
55.19:
God hears, and he will bring them low—
he has been enthroned since time out of mind.
Selah.
Because nothing ever changes them.
No fear of God is in them.
55.20:
He turned against the very ones who trusted him.
He shattered the covenant between them.
55.21:
Smooth—like butter poured out—his talk.
But war was behind every word.
Softer than olive oil, his speech.
But all of it: unsheathed blades.
55.22:
Hand your burden over to the LORD.
He will carry it—he will carry you.
He will never let the righteous be swept away.
55.23:
But you, O God—
you will bring them down into the pit of destruction.
Violent and deceptive people will not live out even half their days.
But I?
I will trust in you.

56.1:
To the choirmaster. To the tune 'Dove of the Far-off Oaks.' A Michtam of David, when the Philistines seized him in Gath.
God, be merciful to me—
they are trampling me underfoot all day long,
fighting against me without stopping.
56.2:
My enemies are all over me, day after day.
There are so many of them, and their pride drives the attack.
56.3:
The moment fear hits—
I choose to trust you.
56.4:
In God—I praise his word.
In God—I trust.
I will not be afraid.
What, in the end, can a mortal do to me?
56.5:
Every day they misrepresent my words, twist them.
Every thought in their heads about me is bent toward my destruction.
56.6:
They huddle together to plot.
They crouch in hiding.
They track every step I take,
waiting for their moment to take my life.
56.7:
Should they just get away with it?
No—in your anger, God, bring down the nations.
56.8:
You have kept track of all my wandering.
Every tear I've wept—collect them.
Have you not written them all down in your book?
56.9:
The day I call out—that is the day my enemies retreat.
This I know with certainty:
God is for me.
56.10:
In God—whose word I celebrate.
In the LORD—whose word I celebrate.
56.11:
In God I trust.
I will not be afraid.
What can any human being ultimately do to me?
56.12:
God, I owe you this:
vows I made, I will keep.
I will bring thanksgiving offerings to you.
56.13:
You have delivered my whole self from death.
You kept my feet from slipping.
