They conspire against the lives of the upright
and condemn the innocent.
94.22:
But the LORD has become my fortress.
My God — the rock where I shelter.
94.23:
He will turn their own evil back on them.
He will cut them off for what they have done.
The LORD our God will destroy them.

95.1:
Come — let us sing to the LORD!
Let us shout aloud to the Rock who saves us!
95.2:
Let us come before him with gratitude—
celebrating him with songs.
95.3:
For the LORD is a great God—
a great King above everything that calls itself a god.
95.4:
The depths of the earth are in his hand.
The mountain summits are his.
95.5:
The sea is his — he made it.
His hands shaped every stretch of dry ground.
95.6:
Come — let us bow down and worship.
Let us kneel before the LORD who made us.
95.7:
He is our God.
We are the people of his pasture,
the flock in his care.
Today — if you will hear his voice:
95.8:
'Do not harden your hearts the way your fathers did at Meribah—
the way they did that day at Massah in the desert.'
95.9:
'They tested me there — put me to the proof—
even after they had seen everything I did.'
95.10:
'For forty years that generation wore out my patience.
I said: these people's hearts are always wandering—
they have never truly known my ways.'
95.11:
'So I swore in my anger:
They will not enter my rest.'

96.1:
Sing to the LORD a new song!
Sing to the LORD, all the earth!
96.2:
Sing to the LORD.
Praise his name.
Proclaim his salvation—
day after day, without end.
96.3:
Declare his glory to every nation.
His wonders — to every people.
96.4:
The LORD is great—
worthy of all praise—
to be held in awe above everything called a god.
96.5:
All the gods of the nations are empty things.
But the LORD made the heavens.
96.6:
Honor and majesty walk before him.
Strength and beauty fill his sanctuary.
96.7:
Give to the LORD, all you families of the nations—
give to the LORD glory and power.
96.8:
Give to the LORD the glory his name deserves.
Bring an offering and come into his courts.
96.9:
Bow before the LORD in the beauty of holiness.
Let all the earth tremble at his presence.
96.10:
Declare it among the nations: The LORD reigns!
The world stands firm — unshakeable.
He will judge every people with equity.
96.11:
Let the heavens rejoice.
Let the earth be glad.
Let the sea roar—
and everything that lives in it.
96.12:
Let the fields burst with joy—
everything in them exulting.
Let all the forest trees shout for gladness
96.13:
before the LORD — for he is coming.
He is coming to judge the earth.
He will judge the world in righteousness,
and the peoples in his faithfulness.

103.1:
A Psalm of David.
Bless the LORD, O my soul—
let everything within me
bless his holy name!
103.2:
Bless the LORD, O my soul—
and never forget
a single thing he has done for you:
103.3:
he who forgives every sin you have committed,
who heals every disease that afflicts you,
103.4:
who pulls your life back from the pit,
who crowns you — not with condemnation —
but with steadfast love and deep compassion,
103.5:
who fills you with all that is good—
so that your youth rises again,
soaring like an eagle.
103.6:
The LORD takes up the cause of the oppressed—
he acts with righteousness and justice
for everyone who has been wronged.
103.7:
He showed Moses how he works — his ways, his character.
He let Israel see what he does.
103.8:
The LORD is compassionate and gracious—
slow to anger,
overflowing with steadfast love.
103.9:
He does not keep pressing the case against us.
He does not hold his anger
without end.
103.10:
He has not dealt with us the way our sins deserve.
He has not given back to us
what our iniquities have earned.
103.11:
As high as the heavens tower above the earth—
that is how great his steadfast love is
for those who fear him.
103.12:
As far as the east is from the west—
that far has he removed our transgressions.
Gone. Not merely covered.
103.13:
Like a father who holds his children with tenderness—
that is how the LORD holds
those who fear him.
103.14:
He knows the stuff we are made from—
he has not forgotten
that we are dust.
103.16:
the wind passes over it — and it is gone.
The very ground where it stood
remembers it no more.
103.17:
But the steadfast love of the LORD —
it rests on those who fear him,
from age to age, without end.
His righteousness reaches down
to grandchildren and beyond,
103.18:
to those who hold to his covenant,
who keep his commands close
and live them out.
103.19:
The LORD has set his throne in the heavens.
His kingdom reaches over everything.
103.20:
Bless the LORD — all you his angels,
his mighty ones who carry out his word,
quick to obey the moment they hear it!
103.21:
Bless the LORD, all his heavenly armies—
all who serve him
and do exactly what he desires!
103.22:
Bless the LORD — all his works,
in every place his dominion reaches.
Bless the LORD, O my soul!

104.1:
Bless the LORD, O my soul!
LORD my God — how great you are,
robed in honor and majesty!
104.2:
You wrap yourself in light as in a robe.
You unfurl the heavens
like a great tent.
104.3:
He lays the rafters of his heavenly halls upon the waters above.
The clouds are his chariot.
He rides the wings of the wind.
104.4:
He sends the winds as his messengers.
Fire and flame — they do his bidding.
104.5:
He set the earth on its foundations—
it will not be moved,
ever.
104.6:
You covered it in the deep — a garment of flood-water.
The waters stood above the mountains.
104.7:
At a single word from you — they fled.
At the sound of your thunder
they ran.
104.8:
The mountains rose.
The valleys dropped down—
each to the place you had chosen for them.
104.9:
You fixed a boundary the waters cannot cross.
They will never again
flood the earth.
104.10:
He sends springs into the valleys—
streams that wind their way
between the hills,
104.11:
giving drink to every wild creature.
The wild donkeys come
and drink their fill.
104.12:
Along those banks the birds of the sky settle in—
singing from the branches.
104.13:
From his high chambers he rains down on the mountains.
The earth is filled
with all that he makes.
104.14:
He makes the grass grow for the cattle
and plants for people to cultivate—
so they can draw food from the earth:
104.15:
wine to make the human heart glad,
oil to make the face shine bright,
bread to give the body strength.
104.16:
The great trees of the LORD drink their fill—
the cedars of Lebanon
that he planted with his own hands.
104.17:
There the birds make their nests.
The stork — she has her home
in the great cypresses.
104.18:
The high mountains were made for the wild goats.
The rocky crags
are a fortress for the rock badgers.
104.19:
He made the moon to govern the seasons.
The sun knows precisely
when to go down.
104.20:
You bring on the darkness — night falls—
and every creature of the forest
comes out to move about.
104.21:
The young lions roar after their prey—
they too are looking to God
for their meal.
104.22:
When the sun rises, they steal back
and lie down
in their dens.
104.23:
And then — the people go out.
They work through the day
until evening brings them home.
104.24:
LORD — how many are your works!
You made them all in wisdom.
The earth is filled to the brim
with what you have made.
104.25:
And the sea — that great, wide sea—
teeming beyond all counting
with creatures great and small.
104.26:
The ships sail across it.
And there — Leviathan,
the great sea creature you made
to play in the deep.
104.27:
They all look to you—
watching, waiting
for their food at the right time.
104.28:
When you give — they gather.
When you open your hand—
they are filled with good things.
104.29:
If you hide your face — they are undone.
Take back the breath within them—
they die and return to dust.
104.30:
But send forth your Spirit—
they are created.
You renew the whole face of the earth.
104.31:
May the glory of the LORD endure forever.
May the LORD take delight
in everything he has made!
104.32:
He looks at the earth — it trembles.
He touches the mountains—
they pour out smoke.
104.33:
I will sing to the LORD as long as I live.
I will make music to my God
for as long as there is breath in me.
104.34:
May my meditation be sweet to him.
I will find my joy
in the LORD.
104.35:
Let sinners be swept from the earth.
Let the wicked be no more.
Bless the LORD, O my soul!
Hallelujah!

97.1:
The LORD reigns!
Let the earth break into joy.
Let every distant coastland be glad.
97.2:
Clouds and deep darkness circle him.
But his throne rests on righteousness and justice—
the moral order is what holds.
97.3:
Fire goes before him.
It burns his enemies
on every side.
97.4:
His lightning blazes across the world.
The earth sees it
and shudders.
97.5:
The mountains melt like wax
before the LORD—
before the Lord of all the earth.
97.6:
The heavens are announcing his righteousness.
Every people on earth
is seeing his glory.
97.7:
Shame on everyone who worships carved images,
who boasts in things of nothing.
Every god bows down before him.
97.8:
Zion heard it and was glad.
The towns of Judah broke into rejoicing
because of your judgments, LORD.
97.9:
You, LORD, are lifted highest over all the earth.
You are exalted far above every god.
97.10:
Love the LORD — and hate evil.
He guards the lives of those who are faithful to him.
He pulls them free from the grip of the wicked.
97.11:
Light has been seeded in the ground for the righteous—
it is growing.
Gladness waits for those with honest hearts.
97.12:
Rejoice in the LORD, you righteous.
Give thanks to his holy name.

98.1:
A Psalm.
Sing to the LORD a new song—
he has done remarkable things.
With his own right hand,
with his holy arm,
he won the victory himself.
98.2:
The LORD has made his salvation known.
He let the nations see his righteousness
with their own eyes.
98.3:
He remembered his steadfast love—
his faithfulness to the house of Israel.
And now the farthest ends of the earth
have seen the salvation of our God.
98.4:
Shout for joy to the LORD — all the earth!
Burst out in song and sing his praises!
98.5:
Praise him with the harp—
with harp and voice together,
melody joined to melody.
98.6:
With trumpets and the ram's horn blast—
shout for joy before the King, the LORD!
98.7:
Let the sea roar with everything in it.
Let the world join in—
and all who live here.
98.8:
Let the rivers clap their hands.
Let the mountains sing together—
for joy!
98.9:
— before the LORD!
For he is coming to judge the earth.
He will rule the world with righteousness
and the peoples with perfect fairness.

99.1:
The LORD reigns!
Let the peoples tremble.
He sits enthroned above the cherubim—
let the earth quake.
99.2:
The LORD is great in Zion.
He is exalted above every people.
99.3:
Let them praise your name—
great and awe-inspiring.
Holy is he!
99.4:
A king's true strength is love of justice.
You established equity.
You executed justice and righteousness in Jacob.
99.5:
Exalt the LORD our God!
Bow down before his footstool—
holy is he!
99.6:
Moses and Aaron stood among his priests.
Samuel was among those who called on his name.
They called to the LORD—
and he answered.
99.7:
He spoke to them from the pillar of cloud.
They kept what he gave them—
his testimonies, his decrees.
99.8:
LORD our God — you answered them.
You were a God who forgave.
Yet you called their wrongs to account.
99.9:
Exalt the LORD our God!
Worship at his holy mountain—
for the LORD our God is holy!

100.1:
A Psalm for the thanksgiving offering.
Shout for joy to the LORD — all the earth!
100.2:
Serve the LORD with gladness.
Come before him with singing.
100.3:
Know this: the LORD is God.
He made us — we belong to him.
We are his people, the flock of his pasture.
100.4:
Enter his gates with thanksgiving.
Come into his courts with praise.
Thank him. Bless his name.
100.5:
The LORD is good.
His steadfast love endures forever.
His faithfulness reaches to every generation.

101.1:
A Psalm of David.
Of steadfast love and justice I will sing—
to you, LORD, I will make music.
101.2:
I will walk the path of integrity.
O when will you come to me?
Inside my own house I will walk
with a whole and blameless heart.
101.3:
I will set nothing vile before my eyes.
The ways of those who turn away — I hate them.
They will not take hold of me.
101.4:
A twisted heart — let it stay far from me.
Evil — I will not know it.
101.5:
Whoever slanders a neighbor in secret—
I will put an end to them.
The proud look, the arrogant heart—
not in my house.
101.6:
My eyes will be on the faithful of the land—
those are the ones I want near me.
Only those who walk with integrity
will stand in my service.
101.7:
No one who deals in deceit
will have a place in my house.
No one who speaks lies
will stand before my eyes.
101.8:
Each morning I will clear out the wicked from the land—
cutting off every evildoer
from the city of the LORD.

102.1:
A Prayer of one who is afflicted—
overwhelmed, pouring out his heart before the LORD.
Hear my prayer, LORD.
Let my cry come before you.
102.2:
Do not hide your face from me
in the day of my distress.
Bend your ear toward me.
Answer me quickly—
the day I call.
102.3:
My days disappear like smoke.
My bones burn
like a heap of coals.
102.4:
My heart is beaten down like grass and withered.
I have forgotten to eat.
102.5:
My groaning never stops—
my bones are grinding against my skin.
102.6:
I have become a bird of the desolate places—
a solitary owl among the rubble.
102.7:
I lie awake.
A lone sparrow on a rooftop—
that is what I am.
102.8:
My enemies taunt me all day long.
Those who mock me
use my name as a curse.
102.9:
I eat ashes like bread.
I mix my tears into my drink.
102.10:
Because of your fury.
Because of your wrath.
You lifted me up—
and threw me down.
102.11:
My days are an evening shadow stretched long before it vanishes.
I am withering like grass.
102.12:
But you, LORD—
you are enthroned forever.
Your name endures
through every generation.
102.13:
You will arise and have mercy on Zion.
The time to favor her has come.
The appointed moment is here.
102.14:
Your servants love even her rubble.
They grieve over the dust of her ruins.
102.15:
When that happens the nations will fear the name of the LORD.
Every king on earth will stand in awe of his glory.
102.16:
For the LORD is building Zion again.
He will appear — in his full glory.
102.17:
He turns toward the prayer of the stripped-bare.
He does not brush it aside.
102.18:
Write this down for the generation to come—
so that a people not yet born
will praise the LORD.
102.19:
He looked down from the height of his holiness.
From heaven the LORD bent his gaze to the earth—
102.20:
— to hear the prisoner's groan,
to set free those marked for death,
102.21:
so that in Zion the name of the LORD would be declared—
his praise ringing out in Jerusalem—
102.22:
— when peoples gather,
kingdoms together,
to serve the LORD.
102.23:
He broke my strength at the midpoint of my journey.
He cut my days short.
102.24:
I said,
'O my God — do not take me away at half my days.
Your years span every generation;
mine are barely begun.'
102.25:
Long ago you laid the earth's foundation.
The heavens are the work of your hands.
102.26:
They will perish — but you will remain.
They will wear out like an old cloak.
You will roll them up and change them like a robe,
and they will pass away—
102.27:
— but you are the same.
You are unchanging.
Your years have no end.
102.28:
The children of your servants will remain.
Their descendants will be planted firmly
before you.

105.1:
Give thanks to the LORD!
Call upon his name!
Tell the nations what he has done!
105.2:
Sing to him — praise him with song!
Dwell on all his wonders.
105.3:
Boast in his holy name!
Let all who seek the LORD
rejoice deep in their hearts!
105.4:
Seek the LORD — seek his strength.
Seek his face without ceasing.
105.5:
Remember what he has done:
his wonders, his marvels,
the judgments that came from his mouth,
105.6:
you who descend from Abraham his servant—
children of Jacob, his chosen ones!
105.7:
He is the LORD our God.
His justice reaches to the ends of the earth.
105.9:
the covenant he cut with Abraham,
the oath he swore to Isaac,
105.10:
which he made binding as law for Jacob—
an everlasting covenant
for Israel.
105.11:
'To you I will give the land of Canaan—
your allotted inheritance.'
105.12:
When they were barely a handful of people,
wandering strangers
in the land he had promised them,
105.13:
moving from people to people,
from one kingdom to another—
105.14:
God let no one oppress them.
He rebuked kings on their account:
105.15:
'Do not touch my anointed ones.
Do not harm my prophets.'
105.16:
He called down famine on the land—
broke every staff of bread.
105.17:
He sent a man ahead of them:
Joseph — sold into slavery.
105.18:
They hurt his feet with chains.
The iron entered into his very being.
105.19:
It continued — until the word came true.
All that time, the word of the LORD
was testing him, refining him.
105.20:
The king gave the order and freed him—
the ruler of nations
set him at liberty.
105.21:
He made him master of the palace,
ruler over everything he owned—
105.22:
with power to direct the king's nobles as he wished
and to teach wisdom
to the men of the court.
105.23:
And then Israel came to Egypt—
Jacob living as a stranger
in the land of Ham.
105.24:
There God made his people multiply beyond counting—
stronger than all
who threatened them.
105.25:
He turned Egypt's heart to hate his people—
brought them to plot
against his servants.
105.26:
He sent Moses — his servant —
and Aaron, the one he had chosen.
105.27:
They performed his signs throughout Egypt—
wonders in the land of Ham.
105.28:
He sent darkness—
plunged the land into night.
And they did not defy his word.
105.29:
He turned their waters to blood
and killed every fish in them.
105.30:
Frogs swarmed through the land—
even into the bedchambers
of their kings.
105.31:
He spoke—
and flies and biting gnats swarmed
through every region.
105.32:
He turned their rain to hail—
and sent blazing fire
flashing through their land.
105.33:
He struck their vines — their fig trees —
and shattered every tree
throughout the land.
105.34:
He spoke—
and the locusts came in,
caterpillars beyond all counting—
105.35:
devouring every green thing in the land,
stripping all the fruit
from every field.
105.36:
He struck down every firstborn in their land—
the best and first
of all their strength.
105.37:
He brought them out — carrying silver and gold.
Not one among all their tribes
stumbled or fell.
105.38:
Egypt was glad to see them go—
the dread of this people
had overwhelmed them.
105.39:
He spread out a cloud over them for shelter—
and fire to light the darkness
through the night.
105.40:
They asked — he brought quail.
He satisfied them
with bread from heaven.
105.41:
He split open the rock—
water gushed out
and ran like a river through the desert.
105.42:
All of this — because he remembered
his holy promise
to Abraham his servant.
105.43:
He brought his people out — with joy,
his chosen ones —
with singing.
105.44:
He gave them the lands of the nations—
they inherited what others
had spent their lives building—
105.45:
all so that they might keep his statutes
and live by his laws.
Hallelujah!

107.1:
Give thanks to the LORD — he is good.
His steadfast love endures forever.
107.4:
Some wandered in the desert—
no road, no direction,
no city to call home.
107.5:
Hungry and thirsty,
their soul
fainted within them.
107.6:
Then they cried to the LORD in their trouble—
and he delivered them
from their distresses.
107.7:
He led them by a straight road
until they arrived
at a city to call home.
107.8:
Let them give thanks to the LORD for his steadfast love—
for all the wonders he has done
for the children of man!
107.9:
For he satisfies the soul that longs.
The hungry soul—
he fills it with every good thing.
107.10:
Some sat in darkness — in the shadow of death—
prisoners in iron chains,
bowed down in misery,
107.11:
because they had defied the words of God
and scorned the counsel
of the Most High.
107.12:
So he broke their pride with hard labor.
They stumbled and fell—
no one there to help.
107.13:
Then they cried to the LORD in their trouble—
and he delivered them
from their distresses.
107.14:
He brought them out of darkness,
out of death's long shadow—
and shattered every chain.
107.15:
Let them give thanks to the LORD for his steadfast love—
for all the wonders he has done
for the children of man!
107.16:
For he has shattered the gates of bronze
and cut through
the bars of iron.
107.17:
Some were their own worst enemies—
made sick by the road they chose,
bowed down by what they had done.
107.18:
Their soul turned from every food.
They had come
to the gates of death.
107.19:
Then they cried to the LORD in their trouble—
and he delivered them
from their distresses.
107.20:
He sent his word — and healed them.
Just his word —
and he pulled them back from destruction.
107.21:
Let them give thanks to the LORD for his steadfast love—
for all the wonders he has done
for the children of man!
107.22:
Let them bring offerings of thanksgiving.
Let them recount his deeds
with shouts of joy.
107.23:
Some went down to the sea in ships—
doing their work
on the great and open waters—
107.24:
they saw the works of the LORD—
his wonders
in the deep.
107.25:
He spoke — and the storm wind rose.
It tore across the water
and drove the waves high.
107.26:
Up to the sky — then down to the depths.
Every bit of courage they had
melted in the terror.
107.27:
They lurched and staggered like drunkards.
All their skill — every last bit of it —
swallowed up.
107.28:
Then they cried to the LORD in their trouble—
and he delivered them
from their distresses.
107.29:
He stilled the storm to a whisper.
The waves —
they were still.
107.30:
Then they were glad — the sea was quiet.
And he guided them in
to the harbor they had longed for.
107.31:
Let them give thanks to the LORD for his steadfast love—
for all the wonders he has done
for the children of man!
107.32:
Let them lift him high in the congregation—
let them praise him
before the council of elders.
107.33:
He turns rivers into desert.
He turns springs of water
into cracked and thirsty ground.
107.34:
Fertile land — he turns it to a salt waste
because of the wickedness
of those who live in it.
107.35:
He turns the desert into pools of water.
He turns dry ground
into flowing springs.
107.36:
He brings the hungry to settle there.
They build a city—
they call it home.
107.37:
They plant fields and vineyards.
They bring in a harvest
rich beyond what they sowed.
107.38:
He blesses them — they multiply greatly.
He keeps their flocks
from dwindling.
107.39:
But then — they are brought low.
Oppression, calamity, grief:
they dwindle.
107.40:
He pours contempt on rulers—
he drives them to wander
in pathless, empty wastes.
107.41:
But he lifts the poor
out of affliction—
and makes their family like a flock.
107.42:
The upright see it and are glad.
Every mouth that speaks wickedness
is shut.
107.43:
Whoever is wise — let them attend to this.
Let them sit with it and understand:
the steadfast love of the LORD is over all.

108.1:
A Song. A Psalm of David.
My heart is steadfast, O God.
I will sing — I will make music
with everything I have.
108.2:
Awake, harp and lyre!
I will be the one
who wakes the dawn.
108.3:
I will thank you, LORD,
among all the peoples.
I will sing your praises
among the nations.
108.4:
Your steadfast love towers above the heavens.
Your faithfulness
reaches to the clouds.
108.5:
Be exalted, O God — above the heavens.
Let your glory cover
all the earth.
108.6:
Save your beloved ones—
reach out with your right hand
and rescue us. Answer us.
108.7:
God has spoken from his holiness:
'I will triumph!
I will divide Shechem;
I will apportion the valley of Succoth.'
108.8:
Gilead is mine. Manasseh is mine.
Ephraim — the helmet on my head.
Judah — my royal scepter.
108.9:
Moab — I wash my feet there.
Edom — I claim it by throwing my sandal.
Over Philistia I shout in triumph.
108.10:
Who will lead me to the fortified city?
Who will bring me
all the way into Edom?
108.11:
Have you rejected us, God?
Do you no longer march out
with our armies?
108.12:
Give us help against the enemy.
Human help is worthless—
only you can save.
108.13:
With God — we will do great things.
For it is he who will tread down
our enemies.

106.1:
Hallelujah!
Give thanks to the LORD — he is good.
His steadfast love endures forever.
106.2:
Who could possibly tell all the LORD's mighty acts?
Who could give voice
to even a fraction of his praise?
106.3:
Blessed are those who practice justice,
who do what is right —
not sometimes, but always.
106.4:
Remember me, LORD,
when you show favor to your people.
Come to me with your saving help —
106.5:
so I may see the good you pour on your chosen,
so I may rejoice with your whole nation —
and join in the praise that belongs to your inheritance.
106.6:
We have sinned — as our fathers sinned.
We have twisted what was straight.
We have done what is wicked.
106.7:
Our fathers in Egypt — they did not take it in.
Your wonders unfolded before them
and they did not think.
They forgot the boundless steadfast love you had shown.
And then, at the very shore of the Red Sea,
they rebelled.
106.8:
And still — he saved them.
Not for their sake. For his name's sake.
So his mighty power would be known in all the earth.
106.9:
He rebuked the Red Sea —
it dried up.
He led them through the deep
as though it were a desert road.
106.10:
He rescued them from the hand of those who hated them.
He bought them back
from the grip of the enemy.
106.11:
The waters closed over their pursuers.
Not one was left.
106.12:
Then — they believed.
And they sang his praise.
106.13:
It did not last.
They forgot — quickly.
They could not wait for him to guide them.
106.14:
In the wilderness, craving seized them —
an appetite that consumed them.
They put God to the test.
106.15:
He gave them what they demanded.
And with it he sent a wasting disease
into their bodies.
106.16:
Then envy spread through the camp —
against Moses,
against Aaron, the LORD's own holy one.
106.17:
The earth opened its mouth
and swallowed Dathan.
It buried the company of Abiram.
106.18:
Fire broke out among them.
Flame devoured the wicked.
106.19:
At Horeb they cast a calf
and bowed themselves down before a molten image.
106.20:
They swapped the glory of God —
their own God, their glory —
for the image of a bull that eats grass.
106.21:
They forgot God — their Savior —
the one who had done
those great acts in Egypt:
106.22:
Wonders worked in the land of Ham.
Terrible acts at the Red Sea.
106.23:
He would have destroyed them —
but Moses, his chosen servant, stood in the breach.
He planted himself between the wrath of God and the people
and turned the destruction aside.
106.24:
Then they despised the land he had promised them —
the good and desired land.
They did not believe a word of it.
106.25:
They sat in their tents and grumbled.
They would not hear
the voice of the LORD.
106.26:
So he raised his hand in solemn oath against them:
they would fall in the wilderness —
106.27:
their children scattered among the nations,
dispersed across the ends of the earth.
106.28:
Then they bound themselves to Baal of Peor,
eating food offered up to the dead —
fellow worshippers of gods who were no gods.
106.29:
They provoked him with everything they did.
A plague broke loose among them.
106.30:
Then Phinehas stepped forward.
He acted.
And the plague was stopped.
106.31:
And this was credited to him as righteousness —
reckoned and recorded,
for every generation, forever.
106.32:
At Meribah they provoked the LORD again.
And Moses suffered for it,
because of them.
106.33:
They had worn down his spirit —
bitter and exhausted.
And so Moses spoke words
he should never have spoken.
106.34:
They did not drive out the peoples of the land —
though the LORD had commanded it.
106.35:
Instead they blended in with the nations around them
and took up their ways.
106.36:
They served their idols.
And the idols became a snare
that caught them fast.
106.37:
They sacrificed their own sons and daughters
to demons.
106.38:
Innocent blood — the blood of their own children —
poured out to the idols of Canaan.
The land itself became polluted
with what they had done.
106.39:
They defiled themselves with every act.
They went whoring after other gods —
betraying the one who had loved them.
106.40:
The LORD's anger burned against his people.
He came to loathe
his own inheritance.
106.41:
He handed them over to the nations.
People who hated them
got to rule over them.
106.42:
Their enemies crushed them.
They were worn down and brought low
under the hand of those who despised them.
106.43:
Again and again he delivered them.
But again and again they rebelled,
following their own stubborn plans —
and were brought down by their own iniquity.
106.44:
And still — he saw their suffering.
He heard their cry.
106.45:
He remembered his covenant on their behalf.
The sheer abundance of his steadfast love
moved him to relent.
106.46:
He even turned the hearts of their captors —
causing them to show compassion
to those they held.
106.47:
Save us, O LORD our God.
Gather us home from among the nations —
so we may give thanks to your holy name
and glory in the praise that is yours alone.
106.48:
Blessed be the LORD, the God of Israel —
from age to age, without end!
Let all the people say: Amen!
Hallelujah!

115.1:
Not to us, O LORD — not to us —
but to your name give glory,
for the sake of your steadfast love and faithfulness.
115.2:
Why should the nations ask,
'Where is their God now?'
115.3:
But our God — he is in the heavens.
He does
whatever he pleases.
115.4:
Their idols are silver and gold—
mere handiwork
of human fingers.
115.5:
Mouths — but they cannot speak.
Eyes — but they cannot see.
115.6:
Ears — but they cannot hear.
Noses — but they cannot smell.
115.7:
Hands — but they cannot feel.
Feet — but they cannot walk.
Not a sound
from their throat.
115.8:
Those who make them will become like them.
So will everyone
who trusts in them.
115.9:
O Israel — trust in the LORD!
He is their help
and their shield.
115.10:
O house of Aaron — trust in the LORD!
He is their help
and their shield.
115.11:
You who fear the LORD — trust in the LORD!
He is their help
and their shield.
115.12:
The LORD has remembered us — he will bless us.
He will bless the house of Israel.
He will bless the house of Aaron.
115.13:
He will bless those who fear the LORD—
both the small
and the great.
115.14:
May the LORD make you more and more—
you
and your children.
115.15:
May you be blessed by the LORD—
the maker
of heaven and earth.
115.16:
The heavens are the LORD's heavens.
But the earth —
he has given it to the children of man.
115.17:
The dead do not praise the LORD.
Nor do any who go down
into silence.
115.18:
But we — we will bless the LORD
from this moment forward
and forever. Praise the LORD!

116.1:
I love the LORD —
he heard my voice,
he heard my cries for mercy.
116.2:
Because he bent his ear down to me,
I will call on him
for as long as I live.
116.3:
The ropes of death wrapped around me.
The grip of Sheol took hold—
I was caught in distress and anguish.
116.4:
Then I called on the name of the LORD:
'O LORD, I beg you —
deliver my soul!'
116.5:
Gracious is the LORD — and righteous.
Our God
is merciful.
116.6:
The LORD watches over the simple.
When I was brought low,
he saved me.
116.7:
Return to your rest, O my soul —
for the LORD
has dealt bountifully with you.
116.8:
You have rescued my soul from death,
my eyes from tears,
my feet from stumbling.
116.9:
I will walk before the LORD
in the land
of the living.
116.10:
I believed — even when I cried out:
'I am in great distress.'
116.11:
In my panic I said:
'Every man
is a liar.'
116.12:
What shall I give back to the LORD
for all the good
he has done for me?
116.13:
I will lift the cup of salvation
and call on
the name of the LORD.
116.14:
I will pay my vows to the LORD —
right there,
before all his people.
116.15:
Precious in the LORD's sight
is the death
of his faithful ones.
116.16:
O LORD, I am your servant —
your servant, born of your maidservant.
You have loosed my bonds.
116.17:
I will bring you the sacrifice of thanksgiving
and call on
the name of the LORD.
116.18:
I will pay my vows to the LORD —
before all his people —
116.19:
in the courts of the LORD's house,
in your midst, O Jerusalem.
Praise the LORD!

117.1:
Praise the LORD, all nations!
Extol him,
all peoples!
117.2:
For great is his steadfast love toward us—
the faithfulness of the LORD
endures forever.
Praise the LORD!

118.1:
Give thanks to the LORD, for he is good—
his steadfast love
endures forever.
118.2:
Let Israel say:
'His steadfast love
endures forever.'
118.3:
Let the house of Aaron say:
'His steadfast love
endures forever.'
118.4:
Let those who fear the LORD say:
'His steadfast love
endures forever.'
118.5:
Out of my distress I called on the LORD.
The LORD answered —
he set me in a wide and open place.
118.6:
The LORD is on my side —
I will not fear.
What can any man do to me?
118.7:
The LORD takes my side as my helper.
I will see
what becomes of those who hate me.
118.8:
Better to take refuge in the LORD
than to trust
in any human being.
118.9:
Better to take refuge in the LORD
than to trust
in princes.
118.10:
All the nations surrounded me.
In the name of the LORD
I cut them off.
118.11:
They surrounded me — surrounded me on every side.
In the name of the LORD
I cut them off.
118.12:
They swarmed me like bees —
blazing out like a thornfire.
In the name of the LORD
I cut them off.
118.13:
They shoved me hard — I was about to fall.
But the LORD
helped me.
118.14:
The LORD is my strength and my song —
he has become my salvation.
(The exodus song of Moses, the song of Isaiah's new exodus — spoken again here.)
118.15:
The sound of joy and victory rings out
in the tents of the righteous:
'The right hand of the LORD acts with power!'
118.16:
The right hand of the LORD is lifted high!
The right hand of the LORD
acts with power!
118.17:
I will not die — I will live.
I will declare
the deeds of the LORD.
118.18:
The LORD disciplined me severely —
but he has not given me over
to death.
118.19:
Open to me the gates of righteousness —
I will enter through them
and give thanks to the LORD.
118.20:
This is the gate of the LORD.
The righteous —
they shall enter through it.
118.21:
I thank you — you answered me.
You have become
my salvation.
118.22:
The stone the builders discarded —
it has become
the cornerstone.
118.23:
This is the LORD's doing.
It is marvelous
in our eyes.
118.24:
This is the day the LORD has made.
Let us rejoice —
let us be glad in it.
118.25:
Hosanna — save us now, O LORD!
O LORD — hosanna!
Send us your blessing and success.
118.26:
Blessed is he who comes in the name of the LORD!
We bless you
from the house of the LORD.
118.27:
The LORD is God — he has shone his light upon us.
Bind the festal sacrifice with cords,
draw it all the way to the altar's horns.
118.28:
You are my God — I will give you thanks.
You are my God —
I will exalt you.
118.29:
Give thanks to the LORD, for he is good —
his steadfast love
endures forever.

109.1:
A Psalm of David.
O God, whom I praise —
do not hold your peace!
109.2:
Wicked mouths, lying mouths —
they have opened wide against me.
They speak nothing but deceit.
109.3:
They surround me with words of hatred.
They fight against me —
for no reason at all.
109.4:
I loved them — and they became my accusers.
I gave myself to prayer
while they sharpened their case against me.
109.5:
Good — they repaid it with evil.
Love —
they repaid it with hatred.
109.6:
Appoint a wicked man to stand over him.
Let an adversary in court
take his place at his right hand.
109.7:
When he comes before the judge — let him be condemned.
Even his prayer —
let it be held against him.
109.8:
Let his days be few.
Let another take over
his place of responsibility.
109.9:
His children — let them lose their father.
His wife —
let her become a widow.
109.10:
Let his children wander — begging for bread —
searching through the ruins
of what was once home.
109.11:
Let the creditor take everything.
What he labored for —
let strangers carry it all away.
109.12:
Let no one show him mercy.
Let no one take pity
on the children he orphaned.
109.13:
Let his line be cut off.
Let his name
be erased from the face of history.
109.14:
Let the LORD keep his fathers' guilt on record.
Let his mother's sin
never be erased.
109.15:
Let those sins stay before the LORD — always —
until he cuts off
even the memory of them from the earth.
109.16:
He never thought to show mercy.
Instead, he hunted the poor and the brokenhearted —
driven to destroy them.
109.17:
He loved to curse — let curses fall on him.
He never cared for blessing —
let blessing be far, far from him.
109.18:
He wore curses like a coat.
May those curses soak into his body like water,
into his very bones like oil.
109.19:
Let it be what he wears —
the garment he pulls around himself,
the belt fastened on day after day.
109.20:
This is what I ask the LORD to bring
on those who accuse me —
on all who speak evil against my life.
109.21:
But you — O Lord GOD —
act for me, for your own name's sake.
Your steadfast love is good.
Deliver me.
109.22:
I am poor and needy —
my heart
is pierced within me.
109.23:
I am fading like a shadow at evening —
shaken off
like a locust brushed from a sleeve.
109.24:
My knees are weak with fasting.
My body is skin and bone —
no substance left.
109.25:
I have become an object of mockery.
They see me —
and they shake their heads.
109.26:
Help me, LORD my God!
Save me
according to your steadfast love.
109.27:
Let them know: this is your hand.
You, LORD —
you are the one who has done this.
109.28:
Let them curse — but you, you will bless.
When they rise up against me, let them be put to shame.
Let your servant rejoice.
109.29:
Let my accusers wear disgrace.
Let them be wrapped in shame
like a mantle thrown around their shoulders.
109.30:
With my mouth I will give the LORD great thanks.
I will praise him
in the midst of the crowd.
109.31:
For he stands at the right hand of the poor —
to save them
from those who would condemn them.

110.1:
A Psalm of David.
The LORD's decree to my Lord:
'Sit at my right hand
until I make your enemies
a footstool beneath your feet.'
110.2:
The LORD sends out your strong scepter from Zion.
Rule —
right in the middle of your enemies.
110.3:
On the day you go to war —
your people come to you willingly,
arrayed in holy splendor.
Like dew from the womb of the dawn,
your young warriors gather.
110.4:
The LORD has sworn — and will not go back on it:
'You are a priest forever,
of the order of Melchizedek.'
110.5:
The Lord stands at your right hand.
On the day of his wrath —
he will shatter kings.
110.6:
He will judge the nations —
corpses will fill the earth.
He will crush rulers
across the whole wide world.
110.7:
He drinks from the brook beside the road.
And so —
he lifts his head high.

111.1:
Praise the LORD!
With my whole heart I will give thanks to the LORD —
in the company of the upright,
in the full congregation.
111.2:
Great are the works of the LORD —
pored over by all
who take delight in them.
111.3:
His work is splendor and majesty in full.
His righteousness —
it endures forever.
111.4:
He made his wonders memorable.
The LORD is gracious —
full of compassion.
111.5:
He gives food to those who fear him.
He is mindful of his covenant —
always, forever.
111.6:
He showed his people the power of his works —
giving them the nations' land
as their inheritance.
111.7:
His works are faithfulness and justice.
All his commandments —
trusty, reliable, sure.
111.8:
They stand firm — forever and ever —
done in faithfulness
and integrity.
111.9:
He sent redemption to his people.
He established his covenant forever.
Holy and awesome is his name.
111.10:
The fear of the LORD is where wisdom begins.
All who live by it understand deeply.
His praise endures forever.

112.1:
Praise the LORD!
Blessed is the one who fears the LORD —
who deeply delights in his commands.
112.2:
His children will be a force in the land.
The generation of the upright —
blessed.
112.3:
Wealth and riches are in his house.
His righteousness —
it endures forever.
112.4:
In darkness, light dawns for the upright.
He is gracious, compassionate,
and righteous.
112.5:
Good things come to the one who is generous,
who lends freely
and runs his affairs with justice.
112.6:
The righteous will never be shaken.
They will be remembered —
forever.
112.7:
He is not afraid of bad news.
His heart is steady —
trusting in the LORD.
112.8:
His heart is steady — he will not be afraid —
until the day he looks on his enemies
and sees what has come to them.
112.9:
He gives freely — to the poor.
His righteousness endures forever.
His honor is exalted.
112.10:
The wicked see it — and rage.
They gnash their teeth and waste away.
What the wicked desire —
it will all come to nothing.

113.1:
Praise the LORD!
O servants of the LORD — praise!
Praise the name of the LORD!
113.2:
Let the name of the LORD be blessed —
from now
and forevermore!
113.3:
From sunrise to sunset —
from east to west —
the name of the LORD is to be praised.
113.4:
The LORD is high above all nations.
His glory —
above the very heavens.
113.5:
Who is like the LORD our God —
throned on high,
113.6:
yet who stoops down
to look at heaven and earth?
113.8:
to seat them beside princes —
the princes
of his own people.
113.9:
He gives the barren woman a home —
he makes her a joyful mother of children.
Praise the LORD!

114.1:
When Israel came out of Egypt —
Jacob's house
leaving a people of strange speech —
114.2:
Judah became his sanctuary.
Israel —
his dominion.
114.3:
The sea looked — and fled.
The Jordan
turned and ran.
114.4:
The mountains skipped like rams.
The hills —
like young lambs.
114.5:
What ailed you, O sea — that you fled?
O Jordan —
that you turned and ran?
114.6:
O mountains — why did you skip like rams?
O hills —
like young lambs?
114.7:
Tremble, O earth,
at the presence of the Lord —
at the presence of the God of Jacob,
114.8:
who turns the rock into a pool of water —
the flint
into a flowing spring.

120.1:
A Song of Ascents.
In my distress I cried to the LORD —
and he answered me.
120.3:
What will be given to you?
What will be added on top of that,
O tongue of deceit?
120.4:
Sharp arrows of the warrior —
coals of the broom tree,
burning long and fierce.
120.5:
Alas — that I must sojourn in Meshech,
that I must dwell
among the tents of Kedar!
120.6:
Far too long has my soul dwelt
among people
who hate peace.
120.7:
I am for peace.
But when I speak —
they are for war.

121.1:
A Song of Ascents.
I lift my eyes to the hills —
from where does my help come?
121.2:
My help comes from the LORD —
the one who made
heaven and earth.
121.3:
He will not let your foot slip.
Your keeper —
he will not slumber.
121.4:
Look:
he who keeps Israel
will not slumber — will not sleep.
121.5:
The LORD is your keeper —
the LORD is your shade
at your right hand.
121.6:
The sun will not strike you by day,
nor the moon
by night.
121.7:
The LORD will keep you from all evil —
he will keep
your soul.
121.8:
The LORD will keep your going out
and your coming in —
from now and forever.

122.1:
A Song of Ascents. Of David.
I was glad when they said to me:
'Let us go to the house of the LORD!'
122.2:
Our feet are standing
within your gates,
O Jerusalem!
122.3:
Jerusalem —
built as a city
knit together in unity,
122.4:
where the tribes go up —
the tribes of the LORD —
by decree of Israel, to give thanks to the LORD's name.
122.5:
There thrones for judgment are set:
the thrones
of the house of David.
122.6:
Pray for the peace of Jerusalem —
the city whose very name means peace.
May those who love you flourish.
122.7:
Peace within your walls —
security
within your towers.
122.9:
For the sake of the house of the LORD our God —
I will seek
your good.

123.1:
A Song of Ascents.
To you I lift my eyes —
O you who are enthroned in the heavens!
123.2:
As a servant's eyes watch the hand of his master —
as a maidservant's eyes watch the hand of her mistress —
so our eyes are fixed on the LORD our God,
until he has mercy on us.
123.3:
Have mercy on us, O LORD —
have mercy on us!
We have had more than enough of contempt.
123.4:
Our soul is more than full
of the scoffing of the comfortable,
the contempt of the proud.

124.1:
A Song of Ascents. Of David.
If the LORD had not been on our side —
let Israel say it —
124.2:
if the LORD had not been on our side
when people rose up
against us —
124.3:
then they would have swallowed us alive
when their anger
burned against us.
124.4:
The floodwaters would have swept us away —
the torrent
gone over our soul.
124.5:
The raging waters —
they would have gone
over our soul.
124.6:
Blessed be the LORD —
he did not give us
as prey to their teeth!
124.7:
We have escaped like a bird
out of the fowler's snare —
the snare is broken, and we are free!
124.8:
Our help is in the name of the LORD —
the one who made
heaven and earth.

125.1:
A Song of Ascents.
Those who trust in the LORD
are like Mount Zion — immovable, abiding forever.
125.2:
As the mountains surround Jerusalem —
so the LORD surrounds his people,
from now and forever.
125.3:
The scepter of the wicked will not rest
on the land of the righteous —
lest the righteous reach out their hands to do wrong.
125.4:
Do good, O LORD,
to those who are good —
to those who are upright in heart.
125.5:
But those who turn aside to crooked ways —
the LORD will lead them away with the evildoers.
Peace be upon Israel!

119.1:
ALEPH.
Blessed are those whose path is pure—
who walk in the law of the LORD.
119.2:
Blessed are those who keep his decrees
and seek him
with an undivided heart—
119.3:
who do nothing wrong
and walk
in his ways.
119.4:
You have commanded your precepts
to be faithfully kept.
119.5:
If only my ways were set firm
to keep your statutes—
119.6:
then I would not be ashamed
when I look on all
your commandments.
119.7:
I will praise you with an honest heart
when I have learned
your righteous judgments.
119.8:
I will keep your statutes.
O LORD, do not forsake me
utterly.
119.9:
BETH.
How does a young person keep their way pure?
By living according to your word.
119.10:
With my whole heart I have sought you.
Do not let me wander
from your commandments.
119.11:
I have stored your word in my heart
so that I would not sin
against you.
119.12:
Blessed are you, O LORD.
Teach me your statutes.
119.13:
With my lips I have spoken
every judgment
that has come from your mouth.
119.14:
I rejoice in the path of your testimonies
as much as in
all riches.
119.15:
I will meditate on your precepts
and look to your ways.
119.16:
I will delight in your statutes.
I will not forget your word.
119.17:
GIMEL.
Deal generously with your servant
so I may live
and keep your word.
119.18:
Open my eyes
that I may see
the wonders hidden in your law.
119.19:
I am a stranger in this world.
Do not hide your commandments
from me.
119.20:
My soul is consumed with longing
for your judgments
at every hour.
119.21:
You rebuke the arrogant — those who are cursed—
who wander
from your commandments.
119.22:
Remove from me reproach and contempt—
I have kept
your testimonies.
119.23:
Even when rulers sit plotting against me,
your servant meditates
on your statutes.
119.24:
Your testimonies are my delight.
They are my counselors.
119.25:
DALETH.
My soul clings to the dust.
Revive me
according to your word.
119.26:
I declared my ways and you heard me.
Now teach me
your statutes.
119.27:
Make me understand the way of your precepts—
then I will speak
of your wonderful works.
119.28:
My soul is melting with sorrow.
Strengthen me
according to your word.
119.29:
Remove from me the way of falsehood.
Graciously grant me
your law.
119.30:
I have chosen the path of truth.
Your judgments I have set
before me.
119.31:
I have clung to your testimonies, O LORD.
Do not let me be shamed.
119.32:
I will run the path of your commandments
when you enlarge
my heart.
119.33:
HE.
Teach me, LORD, the way of your statutes.
I will keep it
to the very end.
119.34:
Give me understanding so I may keep your law
and observe it
with my whole heart.
119.35:
Make me walk in the path of your commandments—
for that is where
my delight lies.
119.36:
Bend my heart toward your testimonies
and away
from greed.
119.37:
Turn my eyes away from what is worthless.
Revive me
by your ways.
119.38:
Confirm your word to your servant—
the promise made
to all who fear you.
119.39:
Take away the shame I fear—
for your judgments
are good.
119.40:
See how I long for your precepts.
Revive me
in your righteousness.
119.41:
VAU.
Let your steadfast love come to me, O LORD—
your salvation,
just as you have promised—
119.42:
so that I have an answer
for those who taunt me.
I trust in your word.
119.43:
Do not take the word of truth from my mouth—
for my hope rests
in your judgments.
119.44:
Then I will keep your law always—
forever
and ever.
119.45:
I will walk in wide-open freedom—
for I have sought
your precepts.
119.46:
I will speak of your testimonies
even before kings—
I will not be ashamed.
119.47:
I will delight in your commandments—
which I love
with all my heart.
119.48:
I will lift my hands to your commandments, which I love.
I will meditate
on your statutes.
119.49:
ZAIN.
Remember your word to your servant—
the word by which
you gave me hope.
119.50:
This is my comfort in affliction:
your word
has given me life.
119.51:
The arrogant have mocked me without mercy—
but I have not turned away
from your law.
119.52:
I remember your ancient judgments, O LORD—
and I am comforted.
119.53:
Horror seizes me
because of the wicked
who abandon your law.
119.54:
Your statutes have been my songs
in the house
of my pilgrimage.
119.55:
I remember your name in the night, O LORD,
and I keep
your law.
119.56:
This is what I have:
because I kept
your precepts.
119.57:
CHETH.
You are my portion, O LORD.
I have made my vow:
I will keep your words.
119.58:
I have sought your favor with my whole heart.
Be merciful to me
according to your word.
119.59:
When I thought over my ways,
I turned my feet
toward your testimonies.
119.60:
I made haste — I did not delay
to keep
your commandments.
119.61:
The cords of the wicked have wrapped around me—
but I have not forgotten
your law.
119.62:
At midnight I rise to give you thanks—
because your judgments
are righteous.
119.63:
I am a companion
of all who fear you
and keep your precepts.
119.64:
The earth is full of your steadfast love, O LORD.
Teach me your statutes.
119.65:
TETH.
You have dealt well with your servant, O LORD—
just as you
have promised.
119.66:
Teach me discernment and knowledge—
for I have trusted
in your commandments.
119.67:
Before I was afflicted, I wandered.
But now
I keep your word.
119.68:
You are good, and you do good.
Teach me
your statutes.
119.69:
The proud have forged lies against me—
but with my whole heart
I will keep your precepts.
119.70:
Their heart is as fat as grease—
but I delight
in your law.
119.71:
It was good for me to be afflicted—
so that I could learn
your statutes.
119.72:
The law from your mouth is better to me
than thousands of pieces
of gold and silver.
119.73:
JOD.
Your hands made me and fashioned me.
Give me understanding
to learn your commandments.
119.74:
Those who fear you will see me and rejoice—
because I have put my hope
in your word.
119.75:
I know, O LORD, that your judgments are right—
and that out of faithfulness
you have afflicted me.
119.76:
Let your steadfast love be my comfort—
according to your word
to your servant.
119.77:
Let your tender mercies come to me so I may live.
Your law is my delight.
119.78:
Let the proud be ashamed—
they treated me wrongly without cause.
I will meditate on your precepts.
119.79:
Let those who fear you turn to me—
all those who know
your testimonies.
119.80:
Let my heart be blameless in your statutes—
so that I will not be ashamed.
119.81:
CAPH.
My soul is faint with longing for your salvation—
but I hope
in your word.
119.82:
My eyes are worn out looking for your word—
I keep asking:
when will you comfort me?
119.83:
I am like a wineskin dried in the smoke—
yet I have not forgotten
your statutes.
119.84:
How long must your servant wait?
When will you execute judgment
on those who pursue me?
119.85:
The arrogant have dug pits for me—
people who have no regard
for your law.
119.86:
All your commandments are faithful.
They are persecuting me without cause—
help me!
119.87:
They nearly wiped me from the earth—
but I did not forsake
your precepts.
119.88:
Give me life according to your steadfast love—
then I will keep
the testimony of your mouth.
119.89:
LAMED.
Forever, O LORD, your word is settled—
firmly fixed
in the heavens.
119.90:
Your faithfulness spans every generation.
You established the earth
and it stands firm.
119.91:
All things continue to this day
according to your decrees—
for all things are your servants.
119.92:
If your law had not been my delight,
I would have perished
in my affliction.
119.93:
I will never forget your precepts—
for through them
you have given me life.
119.94:
I am yours — save me!
For I have sought out
your precepts.
119.95:
The wicked wait to destroy me—
but I give my mind
to your testimonies.
119.96:
I have seen that all human perfection has an end—
but your commandment
is boundless.
119.97:
MEM.
O how I love your law!
It is my meditation
all through the day.
119.98:
Your commandments make me wiser than my enemies—
for they are always with me.
119.99:
I have more understanding than all my teachers—
for your testimonies
are my meditation.
119.100:
I understand more than the elders—
because I keep
your precepts.
119.101:
I have held my feet back from every evil path
so that I might keep
your word.
119.102:
I have not turned from your judgments—
for you
have taught me.
119.103:
How sweet your words are to my taste—
sweeter than honey
to my mouth!
119.104:
Through your precepts I gain understanding—
therefore I hate
every false path.
119.105:
NUN.
Your word is a lamp to my feet
and a light
to my path.
119.106:
I have sworn an oath — and I will keep it:
to obey your righteous
judgments.
119.107:
I am greatly afflicted.
Revive me, O LORD—
according to your word.
119.108:
Accept, O LORD, the willing offerings of my lips—
and teach me
your judgments.
119.109:
My life is in my hands at every moment—
but I do not forget
your law.
119.110:
The wicked have set a snare for me—
but I have not strayed
from your precepts.
119.111:
Your testimonies are my heritage forever—
for they are the joy
of my heart.
119.112:
I have inclined my heart to keep your statutes—
always,
to the very end.
119.113:
SAMECH.
I hate the double-minded—
but I love
your law.
119.114:
You are my hiding place and my shield.
I hope in your word.
119.115:
Away from me, you evildoers!
I will keep the commandments
of my God.
119.116:
Hold me up according to your word so I may live—
do not let my hope
become shame.
119.117:
Hold me up and I will be safe—
continually turning my attention
to your statutes.
119.118:
You have dismissed all who stray from your statutes—
for their deceit
is pure falsehood.
119.119:
You sweep away the wicked like dross—
therefore I love
your testimonies.
119.120:
My flesh trembles in fear of you.
I stand in awe
of your judgments.
119.121:
AIN.
I have done what is just and right.
Do not leave me
to my oppressors.
119.122:
Stand surety for your servant's good.
Do not let the proud
oppress me.
119.123:
My eyes are failing
as I look for your salvation—
for the word of your righteousness.
119.124:
Deal with your servant according to your steadfast love.
Teach me
your statutes.
119.125:
I am your servant.
Give me understanding
that I may know your testimonies.
119.126:
It is time for you to act, LORD—
they have broken
your law.
119.127:
Therefore I love your commandments
more than gold—
more than the finest gold.
119.128:
I count all your precepts right in everything—
and I hate
every false path.
119.129:
PE.
Your testimonies are wonderful.
That is why my soul
keeps them.
119.130:
When your words are opened, they give light.
They give understanding
to those with no learning.
119.131:
I opened my mouth and panted—
I was longing
for your commandments.
119.132:
Turn to me and be merciful—
as you do for all those
who love your name.
119.133:
Steady my steps according to your word—
let no sin
have dominion over me.
119.134:
Deliver me from human oppression—
and I will keep
your precepts.
119.135:
Make your face shine on your servant
and teach me
your statutes.
119.136:
Streams of tears run down my eyes—
because your law
is not kept.
119.137:
TZADDI.
Righteous are you, O LORD.
Your judgments
are upright.
119.138:
The testimonies you have given are righteous—
faithful
in every way.
119.139:
My zeal has consumed me—
because my enemies
have forgotten your words.
119.140:
Your word is utterly pure—
therefore your servant
loves it.
119.141:
I am small and looked down on—
but I do not forget
your precepts.
119.142:
Your righteousness is everlasting.
Your law is truth.
119.143:
Trouble and anguish have come upon me—
but your commandments
are my delight.
119.144:
The righteousness of your testimonies is everlasting.
Give me understanding
that I may live.
119.145:
KOPH.
With my whole heart I cried — answer me, O LORD!
I will keep your statutes.
119.146:
I cried to you: save me!
Then I will keep
your testimonies.
119.147:
I rise before dawn and cry for help.
I hope
in your word.
119.148:
My eyes are open before the night watch—
to meditate
on your word.
119.149:
Hear my voice, O LORD, in your steadfast love.
Revive me
according to your judgment.
119.150:
Those who pursue evil draw near to me.
They are far
from your law.
119.151:
But you are near, O LORD.
All your commandments
are truth.
119.152:
From ancient times I have known this about your testimonies:
you founded them
to last forever.
119.153:
RESH.
Look on my affliction and deliver me—
for I have not forgotten
your law.
119.154:
Plead my cause and deliver me.
Revive me
according to your word.
119.155:
Salvation is far from the wicked—
for they have not sought
your statutes.
119.156:
Great are your tender mercies, O LORD.
Revive me
according to your judgments.
119.157:
Many persecutors and enemies surround me—
but I have not turned
from your testimonies.
119.158:
I looked at the faithless and was grieved—
because they would not keep
your word.
119.159:
See how I love your precepts!
Revive me, O LORD—
according to your steadfast love.
119.160:
The sum of your word is truth.
Every one of your righteous judgments
endures forever.
119.161:
SHIN.
Rulers have persecuted me without cause—
but my heart stands in awe
of your word.
119.162:
I rejoice at your word
like one who discovers
great plunder.
119.163:
I hate and abhor falsehood—
but your law
I love.
119.164:
Seven times a day I praise you—
because of your righteous
judgments.
119.165:
Great peace belongs to those who love your law.
Nothing causes them
to stumble.
119.166:
I have hoped for your salvation, O LORD—
and I have kept
your commandments.
119.167:
My soul has kept your testimonies—
and I love them
exceedingly.
119.168:
I have kept your precepts and your testimonies—
for all my ways
are before you.
119.169:
TAV.
Let my cry come before you, O LORD.
Give me understanding
according to your word.
119.170:
Let my supplication come before you.
Deliver me
according to your word.
119.171:
My lips will overflow with praise—
because you teach me
your statutes.
119.172:
My tongue will sing of your word—
for all your commandments
are righteousness.
119.173:
Let your hand be ready to help me—
for I have chosen
your precepts.
119.174:
I have longed for your salvation, O LORD.
Your law is my delight.
119.175:
Let my soul live — and it will praise you.
Let your judgments
help me.
119.176:
I have strayed like a lost sheep.
Seek your servant—
for I have not forgotten
your commandments.

126.1:
A Song of Ascents.
When the LORD turned Zion's fortunes around,
we were like people dreaming.
126.2:
Then our mouths were full of laughter
and our tongues rang with joy.
The nations said:
'The LORD has done great things for them!'
126.3:
The LORD has done great things for us.
We are glad.
126.4:
Turn back our fortunes, O LORD —
like the wadis of the Negev
running full after rain.
126.5:
Those who sow in tears
will reap
with shouts of joy.
126.6:
He goes out weeping,
carrying his precious seed —
but he comes home with shouts of joy,
laden with sheaves.

127.1:
A Song of Ascents. For Solomon.
Unless the LORD builds the house,
the builders labor in vain.
Unless the LORD guards the city,
the watchman watches for nothing.
127.2:
Why rise so early? Why sit up so late?
Eating anxious bread?
For the LORD gives to his beloved
the gift of sleep.
127.3:
Look — children are a heritage from the LORD.
The fruit of the womb
is his reward.
127.4:
Like arrows in a warrior's hand —
so are the children
born in your prime.
127.5:
Blessed is the man whose quiver is full of them —
he will not be put to shame
when he faces his enemies at the gate.

128.1:
A Song of Ascents.
Blessed — truly blessed —
is everyone who fears the LORD,
who walks in his ways.
128.2:
What your hands work for,
you will eat.
Blessed you will be —
and all will be well.
128.3:
Your wife — a fruitful vine
in the heart of your home.
Your children — olive saplings
around your table.
128.4:
See — this is how the man who fears the LORD
is blessed.
128.5:
May the LORD bless you from Zion.
May you see Jerusalem prosper
all the days of your life.
128.6:
May you see your children's children.
Peace upon Israel.

129.1:
A Song of Ascents.
'Many times they have set upon me from my youth' —
let Israel say it now.
129.2:
Many times they have set upon me from my youth —
and yet
they have not prevailed.
129.3:
They plowed across my back —
the furrows they cut
ran deep and long.
129.4:
The LORD is righteous —
he has cut the cords
of the wicked.
129.5:
May all who hate Zion
be put to shame
and turned back.
129.6:
Let them be like grass on the housetops —
withering
before it ever grows.
129.7:
No reaper fills his hand with it.
No one gathers it
into sheaves.
129.8:
No one passing by will say:
'The LORD's blessing be upon you!'
'We bless you in the name of the LORD!'

130.1:
A Song of Ascents.
Out of the depths I cry to you,
O LORD.
130.2:
O Lord — hear my voice.
Let your ears be attentive
to my cries for mercy.
130.3:
If you, LORD, were to count every iniquity —
O Lord,
who could stand?
130.4:
But with you there is forgiveness —
and that is why
you are held in awe.
130.5:
I wait for the LORD.
My soul waits —
and in his word I hope.
130.6:
My soul waits for the Lord
more than watchmen wait for morning —
more than watchmen wait for morning.
130.8:
He will redeem Israel
from all
its iniquities.

131.1:
A Song of Ascents. Of David.
O LORD, my heart is not proud.
My eyes are not raised too high.
I do not reach for things too great,
too wonderful for me.
131.2:
But I have stilled and quieted my soul —
like a weaned child
content in its mother's arms.
Like a weaned child:
that is my soul within me.
131.3:
O Israel — hope in the LORD,
from this moment forward
and forever.

132.2:
how he swore to the LORD —
vowed to the Mighty One of Jacob:
132.3:
'I will not go to my house —
nor climb into my bed;'
132.4:
'I will give no sleep to my eyes,
no slumber to my eyelids,'
132.5:
'until I find a place for the LORD —
a dwelling
for the Mighty One of Jacob.'
132.6:
We heard of the ark in Ephrathah —
we found it
in the fields of Jaar.
132.7:
Let us go to his dwelling —
let us bow down
at his footstool.
132.8:
Arise, O LORD, to your resting place —
you, and the ark
of your strength.
132.9:
Clothe your priests in righteousness —
let your faithful ones
shout for joy.
132.10:
For your servant David's sake —
do not turn away
the face of your anointed.
132.11:
The LORD swore in truth to David —
an oath he will not retract:
'From the fruit of your body I will set one upon your throne.'
132.12:
'If your sons keep my covenant —
the testimonies I will teach them —
then their sons will sit on your throne forever.'
132.13:
For the LORD has chosen Zion —
he has desired it
for his dwelling.
132.14:
'This is my rest forever —
here I will dwell,
for I have desired it.'
132.15:
'I will bless her provisions abundantly —
I will satisfy the poor
with bread.'
132.16:
'I will clothe her priests with salvation —
her faithful ones
will shout aloud for joy.'
132.17:
'There I will make the horn of David to bud —
I have ordained a lamp
for my anointed.'
132.18:
'His enemies I will clothe in shame —
but upon his own head
his crown will flourish.'

133.1:
A Song of Ascents. Of David.
Behold — how good, how pleasant,
when brothers dwell together in unity!
133.2:
It is like the precious anointing oil
poured on the head, running down
upon the beard — Aaron's beard —
flowing all the way to the hem of his robes.
133.3:
It is like the dew of Hermon
falling on the mountains of Zion —
for there the LORD commands the blessing:
life for evermore.

134.1:
A Song of Ascents.
Come — bless the LORD,
all you servants of the LORD
who stand by night in his house.
134.2:
Lift your hands toward the sanctuary —
bless
the LORD.
134.3:
May the LORD bless you from Zion —
the LORD who made
heaven and earth.

135.1:
Praise the LORD!
Praise the name of the LORD —
praise him, O servants of the LORD,
135.2:
you who stand in the house of the LORD,
in the courts
of the house of our God.
135.4:
For the LORD has chosen Jacob for himself —
Israel
as his treasured possession.
135.5:
I know that the LORD is great —
our Lord
above all gods.
135.6:
Whatever the LORD pleases, he does —
in heaven, on earth,
in the seas and all their depths.
135.7:
He draws clouds up from the ends of the earth —
makes lightning flash with rain,
brings the wind from his storehouses.
135.8:
He struck down Egypt's firstborn —
both people
and animals.
135.9:
He sent signs and wonders into your midst, O Egypt —
against Pharaoh
and all his servants.
135.10:
He struck down great nations,
slew powerful kings —
135.12:
and gave their land as an inheritance —
an inheritance
for Israel his people.
135.13:
O LORD, your name endures forever —
your memorial
through all generations.
135.14:
For the LORD will vindicate his people —
he will have compassion
on his servants.
135.15:
The idols of the nations are silver and gold —
the work
of human hands.
135.16:
Mouths — but they cannot speak;
eyes — but they cannot see;
135.18:
Those who make them will become like them —
so will everyone
who puts their trust in them.
135.19:
Bless the LORD, O house of Israel!
Bless the LORD,
O house of Aaron!
135.20:
Bless the LORD, O house of Levi!
You who fear the LORD —
bless the LORD!
135.21:
Blessed be the LORD from Zion —
he who dwells
in Jerusalem. Praise the LORD!

136.1:
Give thanks to the LORD, for he is good —
for his steadfast love endures forever.
136.2:
Give thanks to the God of gods —
for his steadfast love endures forever.
136.3:
Give thanks to the Lord of lords —
for his steadfast love endures forever.
136.4:
To him who alone works great wonders —
for his steadfast love endures forever.
136.5:
To him who crafted the heavens by wisdom —
for his steadfast love endures forever.
136.6:
To him who stretched the earth over the waters —
for his steadfast love endures forever.
136.7:
To him who made the great lights —
for his steadfast love endures forever.
136.8:
The sun to govern the day —
for his steadfast love endures forever.
136.9:
Moon and stars to govern the night —
for his steadfast love endures forever.
136.10:
To him who struck Egypt through their firstborn —
for his steadfast love endures forever.
136.11:
Who led Israel out from their midst —
for his steadfast love endures forever.
136.12:
With a strong hand, with an outstretched arm —
for his steadfast love endures forever.
136.13:
To him who split the Sea of Reeds —
for his steadfast love endures forever.
136.14:
Who led Israel through the midst of it —
for his steadfast love endures forever.
136.15:
And swept Pharaoh and his army into the Sea —
for his steadfast love endures forever.
136.16:
To him who led his people through the wilderness —
for his steadfast love endures forever.
136.17:
To him who struck down great kings —
for his steadfast love endures forever.
136.18:
Who slew renowned kings —
for his steadfast love endures forever.
136.19:
Sihon, king of the Amorites —
for his steadfast love endures forever.
136.20:
Og, king of Bashan —
for his steadfast love endures forever.
136.21:
Who gave their land as an inheritance —
for his steadfast love endures forever.
136.22:
An inheritance to Israel, his servant —
for his steadfast love endures forever.
136.23:
Who remembered us in our humiliation —
for his steadfast love endures forever.
136.24:
Who tore us free from our enemies —
for his steadfast love endures forever.
136.25:
Who gives food to all flesh —
for his steadfast love endures forever.
136.26:
Give thanks to the God of heaven —
for his steadfast love endures forever.

137.1:
By the rivers of Babylon —
there we sat down and wept,
when we remembered Zion.
137.2:
We hung our harps
upon the willows
in the midst of that place.
137.3:
There our captors demanded songs from us —
those who had ravaged us demanded joy:
'Sing us one of the songs of Zion!'
137.4:
How can we sing the LORD's song
in a foreign land?
137.5:
If I forget you, O Jerusalem —
let my right hand
wither.
137.6:
If I do not remember you —
let my tongue cling to the roof of my mouth —
if I do not hold Jerusalem above my highest joy.
137.7:
Remember, O LORD, against Edom
the day Jerusalem fell —
how they cried: 'Tear it down! Tear it down
to its very foundations!'
137.8:
O daughter of Babylon, destined for ruin —
happy the one
who repays you as you treated us!
137.9:
Blessed shall he be who seizes your children
and dashes them
against the rock.

138.2:
I bow toward your holy temple
and praise your name —
for your steadfast love, for your faithfulness.
You have exalted your word
above everything you are known by.
138.3:
The day I called — you answered.
You made me bold,
you strengthened my soul.
138.4:
All the kings of the earth will praise you, O LORD —
when they hear
what you have spoken.
138.5:
They will sing of the LORD's ways —
for the glory of the LORD
is great.
138.6:
Though the LORD is exalted on high,
he looks to the lowly.
The proud he knows —
only from afar.
138.7:
Though I walk through the middle of trouble —
you keep me alive.
You reach your hand against the fury of my enemies;
your right hand saves me.
138.8:
The LORD will complete what he has begun in me.
Your steadfast love endures forever, O LORD.
Do not forsake the work of your hands.

139.1:
For the choir director. A Psalm of David.
O LORD — you have searched me.
You know me.
139.2:
You know when I sit, when I rise.
You discern my thoughts
from far away.
139.3:
You trace my every path, my every rest.
You know all my ways
before I walk them.
139.4:
Before a word is on my tongue —
you know it entirely,
O LORD.
139.5:
You hem me in behind, you hem me in before,
and your hand rests
upon me.
139.6:
This knowledge is too wonderful for me —
too high.
I cannot reach it.
139.7:
Where can I go from your Spirit?
Where can I flee
from your presence?
139.8:
If I ascend to heaven — you are there.
If I make my bed in Sheol —
even there.
139.9:
If I take the wings of the dawn
and settle at the far side
of the sea —
139.10:
even there your hand will guide me,
your right hand
will hold me.
139.11:
And if I say,
'Surely the darkness will hide me,
the light around me become night' —
139.12:
even the darkness is not dark to you.
The night shines like the day —
darkness and light are both alike to you.
139.13:
For you formed my inward parts —
you knit me together
in my mother's womb.
139.14:
I praise you, for I am fearfully and wonderfully made.
Your works are marvellous —
my soul knows it through and through.
139.15:
My frame was not hidden from you
when I was made in secret —
skillfully woven in the depths of the earth.
139.16:
Your eyes saw my unformed body.
In your book were written
all the days formed for me —
before a single one had come.
139.17:
How precious are your thoughts toward me, O God!
How vast
their sum!
139.18:
Were I to count them,
they would outnumber grains of sand.
When I wake —
I am still with you.
139.19:
O God — if only you would destroy the wicked!
Away from me,
you bloodstained men!
139.21:
Do I not hate those who hate you, O LORD?
Do I not loathe
those who rise against you?
139.22:
I hate them with a complete hatred —
I count them
my enemies.
139.23:
Search me, O God — know my heart.
Test me —
know my thoughts.
139.24:
See if there is any hurtful way within me —
and lead me
in the everlasting way.

140.2:
Who devise evil in their hearts,
stirring up conflict
every single day.
140.3:
Their tongues are sharpened like a serpent's.
Viper venom
is under their lips.
Selah.
140.4:
Keep me, O LORD, from the hands of the wicked —
from violent men who plan
to trip my feet.
140.5:
The proud have hidden a snare for me —
cords and nets spread along the path.
They have set traps for me.
Selah.
140.6:
I say to the LORD: You are my God.
Hear, O LORD,
my cries for mercy.
140.7:
O Sovereign LORD,
strength of my salvation —
you shielded my head
when battle raged.
140.8:
Do not grant the wicked their wish, O LORD —
do not let their evil plot succeed,
lest they rise in triumph.
Selah.
140.9:
The evil of their lips —
let it fall
upon their own heads.
140.10:
Let burning coals fall upon them.
Let them be thrown into the fire,
into pits so deep
they never rise.
140.11:
Let no slander take root in the land.
Let evil hunt down the violent man
until he falls.
140.12:
I know this:
the LORD will uphold the cause of the afflicted,
the right of the poor.
140.13:
The righteous will give thanks to your name.
The upright
will dwell in your presence.

141.1:
A Psalm of David.
O LORD, I call to you — come quickly!
Hear my voice
when I cry out.
141.2:
Let my prayer rise before you like incense —
the lifting up of my hands
like the evening offering.
141.4:
Do not let my heart be drawn toward evil —
toward doing the deeds of evildoers,
or feasting at their table.
141.5:
Let the righteous strike me — that is kindness.
Let him rebuke me — that is oil on my head.
I will not refuse it.
Yet my prayer is still against their evil works.
141.6:
When their rulers are cast down from rocky heights,
the people will hear my words
and find them good.
141.7:
Like earth turned up by the plow —
our bones lie scattered
at the mouth of Sheol.
141.8:
But my eyes are fixed on you, O Lord GOD.
In you I take refuge —
do not leave my soul bare.
141.9:
Keep me from the trap they have laid —
from the snares
of those who do evil.
141.10:
Let the wicked fall into their own nets —
while I
pass safely by.

142.3:
When my spirit grows faint within me —
you know my path.
In the way I walk
they have hidden a trap.
142.4:
I look to the right — no one acknowledges me.
All escape is cut off.
Not a soul cares for me.
142.5:
I cry to you, O LORD.
You are my refuge —
my portion in the land of the living.
142.7:
Bring my soul out of prison —
that I may praise your name.
The righteous will gather around me,
for you will deal generously with me.

143.1:
A Psalm of David.
Hear my prayer, O LORD;
listen to my cries for mercy.
Answer me — in your faithfulness,
in your righteousness.
143.2:
Do not bring your servant into judgment —
for no one living
is righteous before you.
143.3:
The enemy has chased my soul —
crushed my life to the ground —
forced me to dwell in darkness
like the long dead.
143.6:
I stretch out my hands to you.
My soul thirsts for you
like parched earth.
Selah.
143.7:
Answer me quickly, O LORD —
my spirit fails!
Do not hide your face from me,
or I will be like those who go down to the pit.
143.8:
In the morning let me hear of your steadfast love —
for in you I trust.
Show me the way I should walk —
for to you I lift my soul.
143.9:
Rescue me from my enemies, O LORD.
To you I flee
for cover.
143.10:
Teach me to do your will —
you are my God.
May your good Spirit
lead me on level ground.
143.11:
For your name's sake, O LORD — keep me alive!
In your righteousness
bring my soul out of trouble.
143.12:
In your steadfast love — cut off my enemies.
Destroy all who oppress my soul.
For I am your servant.

150.1:
Praise the LORD!
Praise God in his sanctuary —
praise him in the vast expanse of his power.
150.2:
Praise him for his mighty acts —
praise him for the surpassing greatness
of all he is.
150.3:
Praise him with the shofar's cry —
praise him with lyre
and harp.
150.4:
Praise him with tambourine and dance —
praise him with strings
and flute.
150.5:
Praise him with resounding cymbals —
praise him with crashing cymbals.
150.6:
Let every living breath praise the LORD —
Praise the LORD!

144.1: All praise to the LORD, my unshakeable foundation — he shapes my hands for combat, my fingers for war.
144.2: My faithful protector, my fortress — the high tower from which I am delivered; my shield, the one I run to, who lays the nations at my feet.
144.3: LORD — why does any human merit your attention? Why should mortal humanity occupy your thought at all?
144.4: Humanity is a mere vapor — our days flash by like a shadow crossing the ground.
144.5: Stoop down from your sky, LORD, and descend — lay your finger on the mountains and watch them smolder.
144.6: Throw your lightning bolts and shatter the enemy; loose your arrows and break their ranks.
144.7: Reach down from your heights and pull me free — from the flood that would swallow me, from the grip of alien enemies,
144.8: men who speak nothing but lies, whose handshake is itself a fraud.
144.9: Let me sing you a song that has never been heard before, O God — with the full range of strings I will lift your praise,
144.10: you who hand kings their victories, who have again and again pulled your servant David back from the killing edge.
144.11: Pull me free from these alien enemies — men whose every word is hollow and whose sworn oath means nothing.
144.12: May our sons shoot up like young saplings in the prime of their youth; may our daughters stand like polished pillars carved for a palace hall;
144.13: may our barns overflow with every variety of harvest; may our flocks multiply by tens of thousands across our fields;
144.14: may our cattle be strong and productive, our land unbroken — no enemy breakthrough, no one dragged into exile, no wailing in the public squares;
144.15: How deeply content is the people that lives this way — and most of all, how blessed is the people whose God is the LORD!

145.1: I lift you high, my God, my King — your name deserves blessing without end, now and always.
145.2: Day after day, without exception, I will bless you — your praise is my unending occupation.
145.3: The LORD is immeasurably great — the praise due him is equally without measure; there is no bottom to his greatness.
145.4: Each generation hands down to the next the story of what you have done; each age declares your power anew.
145.5: I will turn over in my mind the brilliant weight of your glory, the wonders you have performed — they are my meditation.
145.6: Others will speak of your fearsome, world-shaking deeds; I will add my voice declaring how great you are.
145.7: The memory of your overflowing goodness will spill from their mouths; they will break into song over your justice.
145.8: He is gracious and full of compassion — never quick to anger, overflowing with covenant love.
145.9: His goodness reaches everyone and everything — not a single creature falls outside his tenderness.
145.10: Everything you have made joins in your praise, LORD — and your faithful people lead the song of blessing.
145.11: They will speak of your glorious reign and bear witness to your power,
145.12: so that every human being may know the magnitude of what you do and the splendor of the realm you rule.
