145.13: No earthly dynasty lasts — but your reign never ends; your authority spans every generation without exception.
145.14: When anyone falls, the LORD is there to catch them; when anyone is crushed under the weight of life, he lifts them upright.
145.15: Every living creature looks to you — and you reliably provide what each one needs at just the right time.
145.16: You open your hand — and that is enough: every creature's deep need is met.
145.17: In every action he is entirely just; in every deed he is entirely good.
145.18: He is close — not distant — to everyone who cries out to him; the only condition is that the cry be genuine.
145.19: What those who revere him most deeply want, he gives; he hears their call and comes to the rescue.
145.20: His love is a shield for all who love him in return — but there is no future for those who refuse to submit to him.
145.21: My lips will carry his praise to the end of my days — and may everything that breathes join in blessing his holy name, now and forever.

146.1: Hallelujah! — my whole inner self, praise the LORD!
146.2: For as long as breath is in me, I will praise him — all my days belong to his praise.
146.3: Do not stake your life on rulers or on any mortal — human beings cannot ultimately save.
146.4: The moment a human exhales his last, he returns to dust — and every plan he carried dies with him.
146.5: How richly content is the person who relies on the God of Jacob — whose every hope is anchored in the LORD his God,
146.6: the creator of sky and earth and sea and everything they contain — the one whose faithfulness never breaks.
146.7: who stands up for those crushed by power, who feeds the starving. The LORD opens prison doors;
146.8: he gives sight to the blind, he lifts those who have been flattened by life, he delights in all who live rightly.
146.9: He protects those without citizenship, sustains the orphan and the widow — but every road the wicked travel, he undermines.
146.10: And this king — the LORD — reigns without term limit. He is your God, Zion, across every generation. Hallelujah!

147.1: Hallelujah! It is simply good to sing to our God — this is what we were made for, the most fitting thing we can do.
147.2: The LORD is the one who raises Jerusalem back up; he is the one who gathers Israel's scattered people home.
147.3: He mends the shattered heart and wraps up every wound.
147.4: Every star in the sky — he has counted them all and knows each one by name.
147.5: Our Lord is immense — his power is unmatched, his wisdom beyond all calculation.
147.6: He raises the lowly and throws the wicked face-down into the dust.
147.7: Come, sing your gratitude to the LORD — let harp-strings carry your praise to our God.
147.8: He pulls the clouds across the sky, sets the rain in motion for the earth, and sprouts grass on the bare hillsides.
147.9: Every creature gets its daily portion — even the young ravens crying out receive their share.
147.10: He is not impressed by military horsepower or human physical strength —
147.11: his delight is in those who hold him in reverence, in those who anchor their hope in his covenant love.
147.12: Jerusalem, lift your praise! Zion, glorify your God!
147.13: He has bolted your city gates against every enemy and blessed the children who fill your streets.
147.14: He brings shalom to every boundary and feeds you on the cream of the harvest.
147.15: He speaks — and his word races across the earth, carrying out his will without delay.
147.16: Snow falls at his word like wool laid over the land; frost is scattered like handfuls of ash.
147.17: His hail strikes the ground like thrown chunks of bread — and who can survive the cold he sends?
147.18: Then he speaks again, and the freeze breaks; he breathes warm wind and the streams begin to run.
147.19: To Jacob he has spoken his very word; Israel has received his laws and covenant standards.
147.20: No other people on earth has been given this — they remain without his word. Hallelujah!

148.1: Hallelujah! From the heights of heaven, from the highest reaches — praise the LORD!
148.2: Every angel — praise him! Every heavenly battalion — praise him!
148.3: Sun and moon, give him praise! Every glittering star, praise him!
148.4: You outermost heavens, praise him! And you waters vaulted above the sky — join in!
148.5: Let everything praise the LORD's name — because at his word it all sprang into existence.
148.6: He set them in place for all time — his word stands like an unbreakable law; not one can depart from its station.
148.7: From the depths of the earth, join the praise — great sea-creatures and all that lurks in the abyss,
148.8: fire and hailstorm, snow and fog, violent wind obeying his orders —
148.9: mountains high and low, fruit trees and towering cedars,
148.10: wild animals and tame, creatures that crawl and birds that soar —
148.11: every king and every nation, every prince and every magistrate —
148.12: the young and the old, men and women, grandparents and small children —
148.13: let everything and everyone praise the LORD's name — his name alone towers over all; his glory fills both earth and heaven.
148.14: He has lifted his people to a place of honor — his faithful ones, the children of Israel, those who live in his nearness. Hallelujah!

149.1: Hallelujah! Let the community of the faithful sing the LORD a song that has never been sung before.
149.2: Israel, take your delight in the one who made you; Zion's children, exult in your King!
149.3: Let them dance his praise and play it on drum and harp — nothing held back.
149.4: He delights in this people — and he crowns the lowly with the splendor of his deliverance.
149.5: May his devoted people shout with triumph in the night — even lying down, they cannot contain their song.
149.6: With God's praise shouted from their throats and sharp swords in their hands,
149.7: they go to execute the LORD's judgment on the nations — to bring the sentence he has decreed upon the peoples,
149.8: to chain every king, to fetter every nobleman in iron,
149.9: to carry out to the letter what has been decreed — this is the high calling, the honor given to all his faithful people. Hallelujah!
